# FitCommerce Static Delivery Acceptance and Architecture Audit

## 0. Re-review Update (2026-04-11, Static-Only)

- This addendum supersedes the original verdict and findings below.
- Re-review scope: static inspection of current backend/frontend implementation and tests only.
- Runtime execution was still not performed in this update pass.

### 0.1 Updated Verdict

- Overall conclusion: Partial Pass

### 0.2 High Findings From Prior Audit: Current Status

1. Pricing semantics (order price sourced from refundable deposit)
- Status: Resolved
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:63, repo/backend/internal/application/campaign_service.go:120

2. Actor attribution in payment/split/merge/auto-close timeline and audit paths
- Status: Resolved
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/http/order_handler.go:128, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:138, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:283, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:399, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:572, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:577

3. Missing timeline provenance for campaign-origin orders
- Status: Resolved
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/campaign_service.go:18, repo/backend/internal/application/campaign_service.go:134, repo/backend/internal/bootstrap/app.go:125

4. Export generation silently swallowing report query errors
- Status: Resolved
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go:298, repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go:300

5. Offline-first mutation path unavailable for catalog/report actions
- Status: Partially Resolved
- Evidence (queue added): repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts:14, repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts:149, repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useItems.ts:75, repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useReports.ts:43, repo/frontend/src/app/providers.tsx:62, repo/frontend/src/app/providers.tsx:82, repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx:134, repo/frontend/src/routes/ReportsPage.tsx:201

### 0.3 Remaining Risks (Post-fix)

1. Severity: High
- Title: Offline replay can drop queued mutations on non-offline errors without operator visibility
- Evidence: repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts:51, repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts:54
- Why it matters: server-side validation/conflict failures during replay are removed from queue, which risks silent data loss and weakens operational traceability.
- Minimum actionable fix: retain failed entries with terminal error state, surface per-entry replay errors in UI, and require explicit user/operator resolution.

2. Severity: Medium
- Title: Offline queued create flow uses synthetic client IDs without reconciliation mapping
- Evidence: repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useItems.ts:126, repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useItems.ts:131, repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx:95
- Why it matters: UI may navigate to a temporary item id that does not match server-created id after replay.
- Minimum actionable fix: persist client->server id mapping after replay and redirect/update caches accordingly.

3. Severity: Medium
- Title: Regression tests do not cover new export failure-path behavior
- Evidence: repo/backend/unit_tests/reports/report_service_test.go:171, repo/backend/unit_tests/reports/report_service_test.go:194
- Why it matters: a critical integrity path is now implemented but unguarded against future regressions.
- Minimum actionable fix: add tests that force queryReportData failure and assert ExportStatusFailed + error return.

4. Severity: Medium
- Title: No frontend unit tests for offline mutation queue and replay
- Evidence: repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-cache.test.ts:1 (query cache only), no matches for queue/replay helpers in frontend unit tests
- Why it matters: new offline queue behavior is high-impact and currently unverified in CI.
- Minimum actionable fix: add tests for enqueue/load/remove/replay success, replay transient offline stop, and replay terminal-error retention behavior.

### 0.4 Static Validation Notes

- Updated backend unit coverage includes new assertions for pricing and actor provenance:
  - repo/backend/unit_tests/orders/order_service_test.go:220
  - repo/backend/unit_tests/orders/order_service_test.go:255
  - repo/backend/unit_tests/orders/order_service_test.go:317
  - repo/backend/unit_tests/campaign/campaign_service_test.go:376

- Important boundary remains: this is a static re-review; no runtime confirmation is claimed.

---

## 1. Original Audit Snapshot (Superseded By Section 0)

- Overall conclusion at original audit time: Fail

## 2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary

- What was reviewed:
  - Prompt and constraints: prompt.md:1, prompt.md:3
  - Documentation and run/test guidance: repo/README.md:3, repo/README.md:182, repo/README.md:257, repo/run_tests.sh:27, repo/run_tests.sh:29, repo/run_tests.sh:45
  - Backend architecture, routes, services, security, jobs, seeds/migrations: repo/backend/internal/bootstrap/app.go:242, repo/backend/internal/http/router.go:128, repo/backend/internal/http/router.go:138, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:63, repo/backend/internal/application/campaign_service.go:118, repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go:298, repo/backend/internal/application/backup_service.go:84
  - Frontend offline behavior and reporting/catalog flows: repo/frontend/src/lib/offline.tsx:11, repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts:48, repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx:78, repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx:137, repo/frontend/src/routes/ReportsPage.tsx:203
  - Static tests (unit + API + frontend): repo/backend/api_tests/lockout_test.go:58, repo/backend/api_tests/rbac_test.go:15, repo/backend/api_tests/member_coach_location_test.go:9, repo/backend/api_tests/report_export_test.go:22, repo/backend/unit_tests/orders/order_service_test.go:220, repo/backend/unit_tests/reports/report_service_test.go:158, repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-cache.test.ts:1
- What was not reviewed:
  - Runtime behavior under real network/browser/container execution
  - Real DB load/performance characteristics
  - Real TLS trust and certificate UX on clients
- What was intentionally not executed:
  - Project startup, Docker, tests, external services
- Claims requiring manual verification:
  - End-to-end offline reconciliation conflict behavior
  - Real backup scheduling and restore integrity across actual filesystem constraints
  - Browser-level UX/a11y and production deployment hardening

## 3. Repository and Requirement Mapping Summary

- Prompt core goal:
  - Offline-first operations and inventory suite for fitness-club group-buy workflows, with strict role controls, operational timelines, procurement loop, reporting/export, security/privacy, retention, and encrypted backups: prompt.md:1, prompt.md:3
- Main implementation areas mapped:
  - Backend API routing and RBAC middleware: repo/backend/internal/http/router.go:128, repo/backend/internal/http/router.go:138
  - Core domain/application services (orders, campaigns, procurement, reports, retention, backups): repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:63, repo/backend/internal/application/campaign_service.go:118, repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go:298, repo/backend/internal/application/backup_service.go:84
  - Frontend role-gated routes with offline cache and mutation restrictions: repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts:48, repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx:78, repo/frontend/src/routes/ReportsPage.tsx:203
  - Static test suites and entrypoints: repo/run_tests.sh:27, repo/run_tests.sh:45

## 4. Section-by-section Review

### 4.1 Hard Gates

#### 4.1.1 Documentation and static verifiability

- Conclusion: Pass
- Rationale: Startup, environment, and test guidance are present and statically coherent with repository structure.
- Evidence: repo/README.md:182, repo/README.md:216, repo/README.md:217, repo/README.md:257, repo/run_tests.sh:27, repo/run_tests.sh:45, repo/backend/internal/bootstrap/app.go:242, repo/backend/internal/bootstrap/app.go:249
- Manual verification note: Runtime operability is not confirmed in this static audit.

#### 4.1.2 Material deviation from Prompt

- Conclusion: Fail
- Rationale: Core business semantics are materially weakened in multiple places: offline-first scope is explicitly reduced to online-only for key operations, order pricing uses deposit rather than sale/rental unit price, and operation logs misattribute actors.
- Evidence: repo/frontend/src/lib/offline.tsx:11, repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx:137, repo/frontend/src/routes/ReportsPage.tsx:203, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:63, repo/backend/internal/application/campaign_service.go:118, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:164

### 4.2 Delivery Completeness

#### 4.2.1 Core explicit requirements coverage

- Conclusion: Partial Pass
- Rationale: Many core requirements are implemented (auth lockout/CAPTCHA, KPI/report/export, procurement lifecycle, backup encryption/checksum). However, critical requirement fidelity gaps remain in pricing semantics, complete timeline provenance, and offline-first behavior.
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/auth_service.go:118, repo/backend/internal/security/password.go:29, repo/backend/internal/domain/report.go:35, repo/backend/internal/application/backup_service.go:109, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:63, repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go:298

#### 4.2.2 End-to-end 0 to 1 deliverable status

- Conclusion: Partial Pass
- Rationale: Repository is full-stack and structurally complete with docs/tests. Static quality risks still allow severe business defects despite buildable structure.
- Evidence: repo/README.md:3, repo/backend/internal/bootstrap/app.go:242, repo/frontend/package.json:10, repo/backend/api_tests/report_export_test.go:22

### 4.3 Engineering and Architecture Quality

#### 4.3.1 Structure and decomposition

- Conclusion: Pass
- Rationale: Layering is reasonable (bootstrap, http, application, domain, store, frontend routes/hooks).
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/bootstrap/app.go:58, repo/backend/internal/http/router.go:34, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:15, repo/frontend/src/app/providers.tsx:1

#### 4.3.2 Maintainability and extensibility

- Conclusion: Partial Pass
- Rationale: Structure is extensible, but key reliability anti-patterns reduce maintainability (silent error swallowing in export generation; actor provenance inconsistencies across operations).
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go:298, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:164, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:328

### 4.4 Engineering Details and Professionalism

#### 4.4.1 Error handling, logging, validation, API design

- Conclusion: Partial Pass
- Rationale: Validation and standardized envelopes are mostly good, but export pipeline suppresses query errors and returns completed jobs, which is non-professional for audit/reporting workflows.
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/http/errors.go:65, repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go:298, repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go:170

#### 4.4.2 Product-like service vs demo shape

- Conclusion: Partial Pass
- Rationale: Product-like breadth exists, but critical behaviors still read as rollout placeholders (online-only notices for offline-first scope).
- Evidence: repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx:137, repo/frontend/src/routes/ReportsPage.tsx:203

### 4.5 Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit

#### 4.5.1 Business objective and constraints fit

- Conclusion: Fail
- Rationale: The implementation departs from core prompt semantics in high-impact areas: pricing model semantics, timeline actor traceability, and offline-first operational expectation.
- Evidence: prompt.md:1, prompt.md:3, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:63, repo/backend/internal/application/campaign_service.go:118, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:164, repo/frontend/src/lib/offline.tsx:11

### 4.6 Aesthetics (frontend-only/full-stack)

#### 4.6.1 Visual/interaction quality

- Conclusion: Cannot Confirm Statistically
- Rationale: Static code review cannot validate rendered spacing, typography consistency, hover/focus behavior, or visual coherence.
- Evidence: repo/frontend/src/routes/DashboardPage.tsx:1
- Manual verification note: Browser-based UI inspection required.

## 5. Issues and Suggestions (Severity-Rated)

### Blocker and High

1. Severity: High
- Title: Order pricing uses refundable deposit as transactional unit price
- Conclusion: Fail
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:63, repo/backend/internal/application/campaign_service.go:118, prompt.md:1
- Impact: Revenue/procurement accounting and customer pricing semantics can be materially wrong; billing-model intent is undermined.
- Minimum actionable fix: Use item unit_price (or billing-model-specific price field) as order unit price, keep refundable_deposit as separate liability field; update DTOs and accounting/reporting rollups accordingly.

2. Severity: High
- Title: Operation timeline and audit actor attribution is incorrect in key flows
- Conclusion: Fail
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/http/order_handler.go:123, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:159, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:164, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:328, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:348
- Impact: Tamper-evident audit purpose is weakened because actions by managers/system can be recorded as if done by order owners.
- Minimum actionable fix: Thread performedBy actor through Pay/Split/Merge/AutoClose paths and write the actual actor to timeline + audit events.

3. Severity: High
- Title: Campaign-created orders miss creation timeline provenance
- Conclusion: Fail
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/campaign_service.go:124, prompt.md:1
- Impact: Staff cannot rely on a complete visible operation timeline for campaign-origin orders; explainability is degraded.
- Minimum actionable fix: Add timeline entry creation in campaign join flow (and include campaign linkage metadata).

4. Severity: High
- Title: Export generation silently ignores report query failures
- Conclusion: Fail
- Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go:298
- Impact: Exports can be marked completed with partial/empty content while underlying data retrieval fails, causing silent reporting integrity defects.
- Minimum actionable fix: Handle queryReportData error explicitly, fail export job status with reason, and return error envelope.

5. Severity: High
- Title: Offline-first requirement is materially narrowed by online-only mutation policy
- Conclusion: Fail
- Evidence: repo/frontend/src/lib/offline.tsx:11, repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx:78, repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx:137, repo/frontend/src/routes/ReportsPage.tsx:203, prompt.md:1
- Impact: Core management actions are unavailable offline, conflicting with prompt’s offline-first operational expectation.
- Minimum actionable fix: Implement offline mutation queue + reconciliation for critical flows, or formally narrow and justify requirement scope in prompt-aligned docs.

### Medium

6. Severity: Medium
- Title: Biometric security path is disabled by default and untested in API suite
- Conclusion: Partial Pass
- Evidence: repo/docker-compose.yml:36, repo/backend/internal/http/biometric_handler.go:30, repo/backend/api_tests/integration_helpers_test.go:146
- Impact: A core security/privacy path is not validated in default delivery posture; production readiness risk remains.
- Minimum actionable fix: Provide a tested enabled-biometric profile and add API tests for register/get/revoke/rotate/list flows.

7. Severity: Medium
- Title: Report service tests do not cover failure-path correctness for export data retrieval
- Conclusion: Partial Pass
- Evidence: repo/backend/unit_tests/reports/report_service_test.go:158, repo/backend/unit_tests/reports/report_service_test.go:169, repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go:298
- Impact: Severe export integrity defects can pass static test gates.
- Minimum actionable fix: Add unit/integration tests that force query failures and assert failed export status + error propagation.

8. Severity: Medium
- Title: Order tests reinforce deposit-based pricing instead of catching semantic mismatch
- Conclusion: Partial Pass
- Evidence: repo/backend/unit_tests/orders/order_service_test.go:220, repo/backend/unit_tests/orders/order_service_test.go:231
- Impact: Existing tests can lock in incorrect pricing behavior.
- Minimum actionable fix: Add tests asserting unit_price semantics by billing model and explicit deposit separation.

## 6. Security Review Summary

- Authentication entry points:
  - Conclusion: Pass
  - Evidence: repo/backend/internal/http/auth_handler.go:25, repo/backend/internal/application/auth_service.go:118, repo/backend/internal/security/password.go:29
  - Reasoning: Lockout/CAPTCHA/session lifecycle and Argon2id hashing are implemented.

- Route-level authorization:
  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
  - Evidence: repo/backend/internal/http/router.go:128, repo/backend/internal/http/router.go:137, repo/backend/internal/http/router.go:138
  - Reasoning: Most endpoints are permission-gated; cancel route uses handler/service-level checks after auth middleware.

- Object-level authorization:
  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
  - Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:95, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:586, repo/backend/api_tests/order_test.go:50
  - Reasoning: Order ownership checks exist, but coverage is concentrated; broader object-level checks should be expanded.

- Function-level authorization:
  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
  - Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:525, repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:164
  - Reasoning: Function guards exist for some operations; actor provenance bug weakens effective audit security controls.

- Tenant/user data isolation:
  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
  - Evidence: repo/backend/api_tests/member_coach_location_test.go:9, repo/backend/internal/security/rbac.go:63
  - Reasoning: Static evidence of location-scoped personnel behavior exists; broader multi-tenant isolation still needs runtime and wider test confirmation.

- Admin/internal/debug protection:
  - Conclusion: Pass
  - Evidence: repo/backend/internal/http/router.go:200, repo/backend/internal/http/router.go:209, repo/backend/api_tests/rbac_test.go:15
  - Reasoning: Admin routes are behind auth and role checks with explicit forbidden-path tests.

## 7. Tests and Logging Review

- Unit tests:
  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
  - Evidence: repo/backend/unit_tests/orders/order_service_test.go:220, repo/backend/unit_tests/reports/report_service_test.go:158, repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-cache.test.ts:1
  - Rationale: Broad presence, but important failure-path and semantic checks are missing.

- API and integration tests:
  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
  - Evidence: repo/backend/api_tests/lockout_test.go:58, repo/backend/api_tests/rbac_test.go:15, repo/backend/api_tests/report_export_test.go:22
  - Rationale: Good coverage for auth/RBAC/main happy paths; gaps remain for biometric enabled flows and audit actor attribution.

- Logging categories and observability:
  - Conclusion: Pass
  - Evidence: repo/backend/internal/platform/logger.go:1, repo/backend/internal/jobs/jobs.go:72, repo/backend/internal/jobs/procurement_jobs.go:39
  - Rationale: Structured logging and job-level diagnostics are present.

- Sensitive-data leakage risk in logs/responses:
  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
  - Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/order_service.go:254, repo/backend/internal/security/audit_helper.go:58, repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go:423
  - Rationale: Note redaction and safe-details handling exist; export masking is field-name based and should be expanded with explicit sensitive-field policy tests.

## 8. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)

### 8.1 Test Overview

- Unit tests and API/integration tests exist:
  - Backend unit: repo/run_tests.sh:27, repo/backend/unit_tests/orders/order_service_test.go:220
  - Backend API/integration: repo/run_tests.sh:29, repo/backend/api_tests/lockout_test.go:58
  - Frontend unit: repo/run_tests.sh:45, repo/frontend/package.json:10
- Test frameworks and entry points:
  - Go test: repo/run_tests.sh:27, repo/run_tests.sh:29
  - Vitest: repo/run_tests.sh:45, repo/frontend/package.json:10
- Documentation provides test commands:
  - repo/README.md:257

### 8.2 Coverage Mapping Table

| Requirement or Risk Point | Mapped Test Case(s) | Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock | Coverage Assessment | Gap | Minimum Test Addition |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 5-failure lockout + CAPTCHA flow | repo/backend/api_tests/lockout_test.go:58, repo/backend/api_tests/lockout_test.go:79 | Expects 423 ACCOUNT_LOCKED and captcha verify behavior | sufficient | None material in static scope | Add brute-force/backoff checks for repeated wrong CAPTCHA |
| 401 and 403 route guards | repo/backend/api_tests/rbac_test.go:7, repo/backend/api_tests/rbac_test.go:15 | Unauthenticated 401 and member forbidden on admin users route | sufficient | Coverage concentrated on selected admin route | Add representative 401/403 tests for procurement/report/export/admin subsets |
| Object-level order isolation | repo/backend/api_tests/order_test.go:50 | Member cannot GET other member order (403) | basically covered | Split/merge/pay ownership and cross-user edge-cases not covered | Add object-level tests for split/merge/cancel/pay permutations |
| Personnel location isolation | repo/backend/api_tests/member_coach_location_test.go:9 | Ops manager forced to own location records | basically covered | Isolation coverage not generalized across all location-bound entities | Add location isolation tests for inventory/procurement/report filters |
| Item publish validation + batch partial failures | repo/backend/api_tests/item_test.go:72, repo/backend/api_tests/item_test.go:140 | Expects PUBLISH_BLOCKED and 207 partial failure counts | sufficient | Inline feedback UX only statically inferred | Add frontend assertions for row-level validation reason rendering |
| Procurement closed-loop + variance resolution | repo/backend/api_tests/procurement_test.go:22 | Create/approve/receive/resolve and landed cost retrieval | basically covered | Missing explicit 5-business-day deadline enforcement tests at API boundary | Add tests asserting overdue escalation and resolve constraints by due date |
| Export ACL and download path | repo/backend/api_tests/report_export_test.go:22 | Download returns attachment; coach forbidden for admin export access | basically covered | No failure-path coverage for data query errors during export | Add export tests with forced DB query failure expecting failed job/status |
| Offline-first behavior for reads | repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-cache.test.ts:1 | Cacheable roots include dashboard/items/orders/admin reads | basically covered | Mutation offline queue/replay not implemented | Add tests for queued offline writes and replay conflict policy |
| Pricing semantics (unit price vs deposit) | repo/backend/unit_tests/orders/order_service_test.go:220 | Tests currently validate deposit-driven price path | insufficient | Tests reinforce wrong semantic behavior | Add tests asserting order.unit_price equals item.unit_price by billing model |
| Timeline/audit actor provenance | repo/backend/api_tests/order_test.go:104 | Asserts timeline count and redacted note text only | insufficient | No performed_by actor correctness checks | Add assertions that manager/system actor IDs are recorded on pay/split/merge |

### 8.3 Security Coverage Audit

- Authentication:
  - Conclusion: sufficient static coverage
  - Evidence: repo/backend/api_tests/lockout_test.go:58, repo/backend/api_tests/auth_test.go:7
  - Residual risk: CAPTCHA abuse-rate limiting not explicitly tested.

- Route authorization:
  - Conclusion: basically covered
  - Evidence: repo/backend/api_tests/rbac_test.go:15, repo/backend/api_tests/dashboard_test.go:9
  - Residual risk: Not all route groups have explicit deny-path tests.

- Object-level authorization:
  - Conclusion: insufficient
  - Evidence: repo/backend/api_tests/order_test.go:50
  - Residual risk: Severe cross-user action defects in split/merge/pay could remain undetected.

- Tenant/data isolation:
  - Conclusion: basically covered
  - Evidence: repo/backend/api_tests/member_coach_location_test.go:9
  - Residual risk: Incomplete coverage outside personnel/location domain.

- Admin/internal protection:
  - Conclusion: basically covered
  - Evidence: repo/backend/api_tests/rbac_test.go:15, repo/backend/api_tests/admin_test.go:7
  - Residual risk: Biometric-enabled path not covered because test config disables it.

### 8.4 Final Coverage Judgment

- Conclusion: Partial Pass
- Boundary explanation:
  - Major risks covered: auth lockout/CAPTCHA, representative RBAC, key procurement/report happy paths.
  - Major uncovered risks: pricing semantics correctness, timeline/audit actor provenance, export failure integrity, biometric-enabled security path.
  - Result: existing suites can pass while severe business/audit defects remain.

## 9. Final Notes

- This report is static-only and evidence-based; runtime success was not inferred.
- Root-cause findings were prioritized over repeated symptoms.
- Manual verification remains required for runtime/offline replay and production deployment hardening.</command-args>

{"file_path": "C:\Users\Hello\.claude\plans\happy-doodling-cosmos.md", "content": "# Fix Remaining Partial-Pass Issues (Audit Re-review 2026-04-11)

## Context

The static delivery audit identified 4 remaining issues (1 High, 3 Medium) after the prior fix pass. All 4 must be closed to reach a full Pass on the affected sections:

1. **[High]** Offline replay silently drops queued mutations on non-offline (server-side) errors — `offline-mutations.ts:51-54`
2. **[Medium]** Offline create uses synthetic client IDs with no reconciliation after replay — `useItems.ts:126-131`, `CatalogFormPage.tsx:95`
3. **[Medium]** No backend unit tests asserting `ExportStatusFailed` when `queryReportData` fails — `report_service_test.go:171-194`
4. **[Medium]** No frontend unit tests for offline mutation queue operations and replay behavior — `offline-cache.test.ts` (query cache only)

---

## Fix 1 — [High] Offline replay silently drops failed mutations

### Root cause
`offline-mutations.ts` line 54: non-offline errors cause `removeOfflineMutation(entry.id)`, permanently losing the queued change with no operator visibility.

### Changes

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts`**
- Add optional fields to `OfflineMutationEntry`: `status?: 'pending' | 'failed'` and `lastError?: string`
- Keep `MUTATION_QUEUE_VERSION = 1` (no wipe); treat entries with no `status` as `'pending'` (backward compat)
- Modify `loadPendingOfflineMutations()` to filter out entries where `status === 'failed'`
- Add `loadFailedOfflineMutations(): Promise<OfflineMutationEntry[]>` — returns only failed entries
- Add `updateOfflineMutation(id: string, updates: { status?: ...; lastError?: string }): Promise<void>` — rewrites the queue with the patched entry
- Add `clearFailedOfflineMutations(): Promise<void>` — removes all failed entries (explicit operator resolution)
- Add `storeIdMapping(clientId: string, serverId: string): Promise<void>` — persists client→server ID mapping under key `'offline-id-mappings'` in the same IndexedDB store
- Add `resolveServerId(clientId: string): Promise<string | null>` — looks up server ID for a known client ID

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts`**
- Import `updateOfflineMutation` and `storeIdMapping` from `offline-cache`
- `executeMutation` `create-item` case: capture response, call `storeIdMapping(entry.id, response.data.id)` after successful POST (entry.id is the synthetic client UUID)
- In the catch block: replace `await removeOfflineMutation(entry.id)` with `await updateOfflineMutation(entry.id, { status: 'failed', lastError: String(error) })` — entry is retained, not removed
- Dispatch `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('fitcommerce:mutations-replay'))` after the loop so the banner component can refresh

**`repo/frontend/src/app/providers.tsx`**
- No functional change needed (return value of `replayOfflineMutations` is already ignored)

**New `repo/frontend/src/components/FailedMutationsBanner.tsx`**
- Reads failed count via `loadFailedOfflineMutations()` on mount and on `fitcommerce:mutations-replay` event
- Renders an MUI `<Alert severity="error">` with a "Dismiss" `<Button>` when count > 0
- "Dismiss" calls `clearFailedOfflineMutations()` and resets local count to 0 (explicit operator resolution)
- Message: `"{n} queued change(s) could not be synced due to a server error. Dismiss to discard."`

**`repo/frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx`**
- Import and render `<FailedMutationsBanner />` directly after `<OfflineStatusBanner />`

---

## Fix 2 — [Medium] Synthetic client ID used as navigation target after offline create

### Root cause
`CatalogFormPage.tsx:95` navigates to `/catalog/${createdItem.id}` even when offline. When offline, `createdItem.id` is the synthetic UUID used as the queue entry ID — a URL that will 404 until replay. After replay, the server creates a different real ID.

### Changes

**`repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx`**
- In the `else` (create) branch of `onSubmit`, split offline/online navigation:
  ```
  // offline: go to list, not to synthetic ID
  if (isOffline) {
    notify.success('Item draft queued. It will sync when you reconnect.');
    navigate('/catalog');
  } else {
    notify.success('Item saved as a draft.');
    navigate(createdItem?.id ? `/catalog/${createdItem.id}` : '/catalog');
  }
  ```
- Edit path is unchanged (ID is always real for an existing item)

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts`** (already in Fix 1)
- `storeIdMapping(entry.id, serverId)` after successful `create-item` replay so later navigation (e.g. from list) can resolve the real server ID if needed

---

## Fix 3 — [Medium] Backend: no test for export query-data failure path

### Root cause
`report_service_test.go` passes `nil` as the pool, and the seeded report type `"items_summary"` falls to the `default` branch in `queryReportData`, returning empty records without touching the pool. The critical error path (lines 299–303) is never exercised.

### Changes

**`repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go`**
- Add unexported field to `ReportServiceImpl`: `queryDataFn func(context.Context, string, map[string]string) ([]map[string]interface{}, error)`
- Add exported setter (test seam): `func (s *ReportServiceImpl) WithQueryDataFn(fn func(context.Context, string, map[string]string) ([]map[string]interface{}, error))`
- In `queryReportData`: check `if s.queryDataFn != nil { return s.queryDataFn(ctx, reportType, filters) }` before the switch — this is the first statement, so production code (nil fn) is unaffected

**`repo/backend/unit_tests/reports/report_service_test.go`**
- Add helper `newReportServiceImpl` that returns `*application.ReportServiceImpl` (not the interface), enabling `.WithQueryDataFn(...)` to be called in tests
- Add two new test functions:
  1. `TestGenerateExport_QueryDataFailure_SetsStatusFailed` — injects a fn that returns `errors.New("db unavailable")`; asserts returned `job.Status == domain.ExportStatusFailed` and `err != nil`
  2. `TestGenerateExport_QueryDataFailure_JobPersistedAsFailed` — same injection; asserts the job exists in the mock export repo with `Status == ExportStatusFailed` (verifies `exportRepo.Update` was called)
- Add `"errors"` to imports

---

## Fix 4 — [Medium] Frontend: no unit tests for offline mutation queue and replay

### Changes

**`repo/frontend/package.json`**
- Add `"fake-indexeddb": "^4.0.2"` to `devDependencies` (lightweight, no prod impact, standard for jsdom IndexedDB testing)

**`repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-cache.test.ts`** (extend existing file)
- Add `import 'fake-indexeddb/auto'` as the first import (polyfills `window.indexedDB` for this test file only)
- Add a new `describe` block: `"offline mutation queue operations"` with tests:
  1. `enqueueOfflineMutation adds entry to empty queue`
  2. `enqueueOfflineMutation appends to existing queue`
  3. `loadPendingOfflineMutations returns only pending entries (not failed)`
  4. `removeOfflineMutation removes the targeted entry`
  5. `updateOfflineMutation sets status=failed and lastError`
  6. `loadFailedOfflineMutations returns only failed entries`
  7. `clearFailedOfflineMutations removes all failed entries, keeps pending`

**New `repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-mutations.test.ts`**
- Mock `@/lib/offline-cache` with `vi.mock` (all queue functions)
- Mock `@/lib/api-client` with `vi.mock` (apiClient.post, apiClient.put, isOfflineApiError)
- Control `navigator.onLine` via `Object.defineProperty`
- Tests:
  1. `returns 0 immediately when browser is offline` (navigator.onLine = false)
  2. `processes create-item entry successfully — removes from queue, increments count`
  3. `processes update-item entry successfully — removes from queue`
  4. `stops processing on offline error — does not remove or fail-mark the entry, breaks loop`
  5. `marks entry as failed on terminal server error — calls updateOfflineMutation with status:failed`
  6. `invalidates items/reports/exports queries after ≥1 successful replay`
  7. `dispatches fitcommerce:mutations-replay event after replay loop`

---

## Verification

npx tsc --noEmit

function.arguments:
{"file_path": "C:\Users\Hello\.claude\plans\happy-doodling-cosmos.md", "content": "# Fix Remaining Partial-Pass Issues (Audit Re-review 2026-04-11)

## Context

The static delivery audit identified 4 remaining issues (1 High, 3 Medium) after the prior fix pass. All 4 must be closed to reach a full Pass on the affected sections:

1. **[High]** Offline replay silently drops queued mutations on non-offline (server-side) errors — `offline-mutations.ts:51-54`
2. **[Medium]** Offline create uses synthetic client IDs with no reconciliation after replay — `useItems.ts:126-131`, `CatalogFormPage.tsx:95`
3. **[Medium]** No backend unit tests asserting `ExportStatusFailed` when `queryReportData` fails — `report_service_test.go:171-194`
4. **[Medium]** No frontend unit tests for offline mutation queue operations and replay behavior — `offline-cache.test.ts` (query cache only)

---

## Fix 1 — [High] Offline replay silently drops failed mutations

### Root cause
`offline-mutations.ts` line 54: non-offline errors cause `removeOfflineMutation(entry.id)`, permanently losing the queued change with no operator visibility.

### Changes

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts`**
- Add optional fields to `OfflineMutationEntry`: `status?: 'pending' | 'failed'` and `lastError?: string`
- Keep `MUTATION_QUEUE_VERSION = 1` (no wipe); treat entries with no `status` as `'pending'` (backward compat)
- Modify `loadPendingOfflineMutations()` to filter out entries where `status === 'failed'`
- Add `loadFailedOfflineMutations(): Promise<OfflineMutationEntry[]>` — returns only failed entries
- Add `updateOfflineMutation(id: string, updates: { status?: ...; lastError?: string }): Promise<void>` — rewrites the queue with the patched entry
- Add `clearFailedOfflineMutations(): Promise<void>` — removes all failed entries (explicit operator resolution)
- Add `storeIdMapping(clientId: string, serverId: string): Promise<void>` — persists client→server ID mapping under key `'offline-id-mappings'` in the same IndexedDB store
- Add `resolveServerId(clientId: string): Promise<string | null>` — looks up server ID for a known client ID

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts`**
- Import `updateOfflineMutation` and `storeIdMapping` from `offline-cache`
- `executeMutation` `create-item` case: capture response, call `storeIdMapping(entry.id, response.data.id)` after successful POST (entry.id is the synthetic client UUID)
- In the catch block: replace `await removeOfflineMutation(entry.id)` with `await updateOfflineMutation(entry.id, { status: 'failed', lastError: String(error) })` — entry is retained, not removed
- Dispatch `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('fitcommerce:mutations-replay'))` after the loop so the banner component can refresh

**`repo/frontend/src/app/providers.tsx`**
- No functional change needed (return value of `replayOfflineMutations` is already ignored)

**New `repo/frontend/src/components/FailedMutationsBanner.tsx`**
- Reads failed count via `loadFailedOfflineMutations()` on mount and on `fitcommerce:mutations-replay` event
- Renders an MUI `<Alert severity="error">` with a "Dismiss" `<Button>` when count > 0
- "Dismiss" calls `clearFailedOfflineMutations()` and resets local count to 0 (explicit operator resolution)
- Message: `"{n} queued change(s) could not be synced due to a server error. Dismiss to discard."`

**`repo/frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx`**
- Import and render `<FailedMutationsBanner />` directly after `<OfflineStatusBanner />`

---

## Fix 2 — [Medium] Synthetic client ID used as navigation target after offline create

### Root cause
`CatalogFormPage.tsx:95` navigates to `/catalog/${createdItem.id}` even when offline. When offline, `createdItem.id` is the synthetic UUID used as the queue entry ID — a URL that will 404 until replay. After replay, the server creates a different real ID.

### Changes

**`repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx`**
- In the `else` (create) branch of `onSubmit`, split offline/online navigation:
  ```
  // offline: go to list, not to synthetic ID
  if (isOffline) {
    notify.success('Item draft queued. It will sync when you reconnect.');
    navigate('/catalog');
  } else {
    notify.success('Item saved as a draft.');
    navigate(createdItem?.id ? `/catalog/${createdItem.id}` : '/catalog');
  }
  ```
- Edit path is unchanged (ID is always real for an existing item)

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts`** (already in Fix 1)
- `storeIdMapping(entry.id, serverId)` after successful `create-item` replay so later navigation (e.g. from list) can resolve the real server ID if needed

---

## Fix 3 — [Medium] Backend: no test for export query-data failure path

### Root cause
`report_service_test.go` passes `nil` as the pool, and the seeded report type `"items_summary"` falls to the `default` branch in `queryReportData`, returning empty records without touching the pool. The critical error path (lines 299–303) is never exercised.

### Changes

**`repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go`**
- Add unexported field to `ReportServiceImpl`: `queryDataFn func(context.Context, string, map[string]string) ([]map[string]interface{}, error)`
- Add exported setter (test seam): `func (s *ReportServiceImpl) WithQueryDataFn(fn func(context.Context, string, map[string]string) ([]map[string]interface{}, error))`
- In `queryReportData`: check `if s.queryDataFn != nil { return s.queryDataFn(ctx, reportType, filters) }` before the switch — this is the first statement, so production code (nil fn) is unaffected

**`repo/backend/unit_tests/reports/report_service_test.go`**
- Add helper `newReportServiceImpl` that returns `*application.ReportServiceImpl` (not the interface), enabling `.WithQueryDataFn(...)` to be called in tests
- Add two new test functions:
  1. `TestGenerateExport_QueryDataFailure_SetsStatusFailed` — injects a fn that returns `errors.New("db unavailable")`; asserts returned `job.Status == domain.ExportStatusFailed` and `err != nil`
  2. `TestGenerateExport_QueryDataFailure_JobPersistedAsFailed` — same injection; asserts the job exists in the mock export repo with `Status == ExportStatusFailed` (verifies `exportRepo.Update` was called)
- Add `"errors"` to imports

---

## Fix 4 — [Medium] Frontend: no unit tests for offline mutation queue and replay

### Changes

**`repo/frontend/package.json`**
- Add `"fake-indexeddb": "^4.0.2"` to `devDependencies` (lightweight, no prod impact, standard for jsdom IndexedDB testing)

**`repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-cache.test.ts`** (extend existing file)
- Add `import 'fake-indexeddb/auto'` as the first import (polyfills `window.indexedDB` for this test file only)
- Add a new `describe` block: `"offline mutation queue operations"` with tests:
  1. `enqueueOfflineMutation adds entry to empty queue`
  2. `enqueueOfflineMutation appends to existing queue`
  3. `loadPendingOfflineMutations returns only pending entries (not failed)`
  4. `removeOfflineMutation removes the targeted entry`
  5. `updateOfflineMutation sets status=failed and lastError`
  6. `loadFailedOfflineMutations returns only failed entries`
  7. `clearFailedOfflineMutations removes all failed entries, keeps pending`

**New `repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-mutations.test.ts`**
- Mock `@/lib/offline-cache` with `vi.mock` (all queue functions)
- Mock `@/lib/api-client` with `vi.mock` (apiClient.post, apiClient.put, isOfflineApiError)
- Control `navigator.onLine` via `Object.defineProperty`
- Tests:
  1. `returns 0 immediately when browser is offline` (navigator.onLine = false)
  2. `processes create-item entry successfully — removes from queue, increments count`
  3. `processes update-item entry successfully — removes from queue`
  4. `stops processing on offline error — does not remove or fail-mark the entry, breaks loop`
  5. `marks entry as failed on terminal server error — calls updateOfflineMutation with status:failed`
  6. `invalidates items/reports/exports queries after ≥1 successful replay`
  7. `dispatches fitcommerce:mutations-replay event after replay loop`

---

## Verification

### Backend
```bash
cd repo/backend
go test ./unit_tests/reports/... -v -run TestGenerateExport
```
Expect: 4 passing tests (2 existing + 2 new failure-path tests).

### Frontend (tests)
```bash
cd repo/frontend
npx vitest run unit_tests/lib/offline-cache.test.ts
npx vitest run unit_tests/lib/offline-mutations.test.ts
```
Expect: all new tests pass. No regressions in existing offline-cache tests.

### Frontend (build check)
```bash
cd repo/frontend
npx tsc --noEmit
```
Expect: no type errors from the new `status`/`lastError` fields, `updateOfflineMutation`, `storeIdMapping`, `FailedMutationsBanner`, or navigation change.

---

## Files Modified / Created (summary)

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts` | Modify — add status/lastError fields, 4 new functions, ID mapping helpers |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts` | Modify — store ID mapping on create-item, mark failed instead of remove, dispatch event |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx` | Modify — navigate to `/catalog` on offline create |
| `repo/frontend/src/components/FailedMutationsBanner.tsx` | Create — error banner with dismiss |
| `repo/frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx` | Modify — render FailedMutationsBanner |
| `repo/frontend/package.json` | Modify — add fake-indexeddb devDependency |
| `repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-cache.test.ts` | Modify — add queue operation tests |
| `repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-mutations.test.ts` | Create — replay logic tests |
| `repo/backend/internal/application/report_service.go` | Modify — add queryDataFn field + WithQueryDataFn setter |
| `repo/backend/unit_tests/reports/report_service_test.go` | Modify — add 2 failure-path tests |
"}

## Approved Plan:
# Fix Remaining Partial-Pass Issues (Audit Re-review 2026-04-11)

## Context

The static delivery audit identified 4 remaining issues (1 High, 3 Medium) after the prior fix pass. All 4 must be closed to reach a full Pass on the affected sections:

1. **[High]** Offline replay silently drops queued mutations on non-offline (server-side) errors — `offline-mutations.ts:51-54`
2. **[Medium]** Offline create uses synthetic client IDs with no reconciliation after replay — `useItems.ts:126-131`, `CatalogFormPage.tsx:95`
3. **[Medium]** No backend unit tests asserting `ExportStatusFailed` when `queryReportData` fails — `report_service_test.go:171-194`
4. **[Medium]** No frontend unit tests for offline mutation queue operations and replay behavior — `offline-cache.test.ts` (query cache only)

---

## Fix 1 — [High] Offline replay silently drops failed mutations

### Root cause
`offline-mutations.ts` line 54: non-offline errors cause `removeOfflineMutation(entry.id)`, permanently losing the queued change with no operator visibility.

### Changes

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts`**
- Add optional fields to `OfflineMutationEntry`: `status?: 'pending' | 'failed'` and `lastError?: string`
- Keep `MUTATION_QUEUE_VERSION = 1` (no wipe); treat entries with no `status` as `'pending'` (backward compat)
- Modify `loadPendingOfflineMutations()` to filter out entries where `status === 'failed'`
- Add `loadFailedOfflineMutations(): Promise<OfflineMutationEntry[]>` — returns only failed entries
- Add `updateOfflineMutation(id: string, updates: { status?: ...; lastError?: string }): Promise<void>` — rewrites the queue with the patched entry
- Add `clearFailedOfflineMutations(): Promise<void>` — removes all failed entries (explicit operator resolution)
- Add `storeIdMapping(clientId: string, serverId: string): Promise<void>` — persists client→server ID mapping under key `'offline-id-mappings'` in the same IndexedDB store
- Add `resolveServerId(clientId: string): Promise<string | null>` — looks up server ID for a known client ID

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts`**
- Import `updateOfflineMutation` and `storeIdMapping` from `offline-cache`
- `executeMutation` `create-item` case: capture response, call `storeIdMapping(entry.id, response.data.id)` after successful POST (entry.id is the synthetic client UUID)
- In the catch block: replace `await removeOfflineMutation(entry.id)` with `await updateOfflineMutation(entry.id, { status: 'failed', lastError: String(error) })` — entry is retained, not removed
- Dispatch `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('fitcommerce:mutations-replay'))` after the loop so the banner component can refresh

**`repo/frontend/src/app/providers.tsx`**
- No functional change needed (return value of `replayOfflineMutations` is already ignored)

**New `repo/frontend/src/components/FailedMutationsBanner.tsx`**
- Reads failed count via `loadFailedOfflineMutations()` on mount and on `fitcommerce:mutations-replay` event
- Renders an MUI `<Alert severity="error">` with a "Dismiss" `<Button>` when count > 0
- "Dismiss" calls `clearFailedOfflineMutations()` and resets local count to 0 (explicit operator resolution)
- Message: `"{n} queued change(s) could not be synced due to a server error. Dismiss to discard."`

**`repo/frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx`**
- Import and render `<FailedMutationsBanner />` directly after `<OfflineStatusBanner />`

---

## Fix 2 — [Medium] Synthetic client ID used as navigation target after offline create

### Root cause
`CatalogFormPage.tsx:95` navigates to `/catalog/${createdItem.id}` even when offline. When offline, `createdItem.id` is the synthetic UUID used as the queue entry ID — a URL that will 404 until replay. After replay, the server creates a different real ID.

### Changes

**`repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx`**
- In the `else` (create) branch of `onSubmit`, split offline/online navigation:
  ```
  // offline: go to list, not to synthetic ID
  if (isOffline) {
    notify.success('Item draft queued. It will sync when you reconnect.');
    navigate('/catalog');
  } else {
    notify.success('Item saved as a draft.');
    navigate(createdItem?.id ? `/catalog/${createdItem.id}` : '/catalog');
  }
  ```
- Edit path is unchanged (ID is always real for an existing item)

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts`** (already in Fix 1)
- `storeIdMapping(entry.id, serverId)` after successful `create-item` replay so later navigation (e.g. from list) can resolve the real server ID if needed

---

105	// TestAuth_CaptchaChallenge_DBRowHasHashNotPlaintext is the postgres-boundary
106	// persistence assertion for G1: it proves that the captcha_challenges table
107	// stores derived verification material (answer_hash + answer_salt) and that
108	// no plaintext answer column exists at the DB level.
109	func TestAuth_CaptchaChallenge_DBRowHasHashNotPlaintext(t *testing.T) {
110		app := newIntegrationApp(t)
111		challengeID := triggerCaptchaChallenge(t, app)
112	
113		// Assert the DB row carries hash + salt, both non-empty.
114		var answerHash, answerSalt []byte
115		err := app.app.Pool.QueryRow(context.Background(),
116			`SELECT answer_hash, answer_salt FROM captcha_challenges WHERE id = $1`,
117			challengeID,
118		).Scan(&answerHash, &answerSalt)
119		if err != nil {
120			t.Fatalf("query captcha_challenges row: %v", err)
121		}
122		if len(answerHash) == 0 {
123			t.Error("answer_hash column must be non-empty in persisted DB row")
124		}
125		if len(answerSalt) == 0 {
126			t.Error("answer_salt column must be non-empty in persisted DB row")
127		}
128	
129		// Assert no plaintext answer column exists in the table schema.
130		var plaintextColumnExists bool
131		err = app.app.Pool.QueryRow(context.Background(),
132			`SELECT EXISTS (
133				SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns
134				WHERE table_schema = 'public'
135				  AND table_name   = 'captcha_challenges'
136				  AND column_name  = 'answer'
137			)`,
138		).Scan(&plaintextColumnExists)
139		if err != nil {
140			t.Fatalf("check information_schema for plaintext answer column: %v", err)
141		}
142		if plaintextColumnExists {
143			t.Error("captcha_challenges table must not contain a plaintext 'answer' column")
144		}
145	}

## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)

### Blocker / High

1. Severity: **High**  
   Title: **Biometric key model is not envelope-safe; key reference is treated as key material**  
   Conclusion: **Fail**  
   Evidence:

- `repo/backend/internal/security/crypto.go:81`
- `repo/backend/internal/security/crypto.go:82`
- `repo/backend/internal/application/biometric_service.go:131`
- `repo/backend/internal/application/biometric_service.go:56`
- `repo/backend/database/migrations/00001_enums_and_base.sql:45`
  Impact:
- If DB encryption key records are exposed, attacker obtains effective derivation input and can decrypt biometric blobs, undermining at-rest confidentiality claims.
  Minimum actionable fix:
- Treat `key_reference` as opaque KMS reference only; never derive encryption keys directly from DB-stored string.
- Introduce proper envelope encryption: DEK generated randomly, wrapped/unwrapped by KEK in HSM/KMS or secure local keystore.
- Store only wrapped DEK or opaque handle in DB.

2. Severity: **High**  
   Title: **Retention deletion is not transactionally tied to audit traceability**  
   Conclusion: **Fail**  
   Evidence:

- `repo/backend/internal/application/retention_service.go:145`
- `repo/backend/internal/application/retention_service.go:151`
- `repo/backend/internal/application/retention_service.go:159`
  Impact:
- Records can be deleted even when per-record audit logging fails, violating "deletions are traceable" guarantee.
  Minimum actionable fix:
- Perform audit-log write and delete in one transaction with fail-closed behavior.
- Abort deletion when any required audit write fails.

### Medium

3. Severity: **Medium**  
   Title: **Dashboard API documentation uses wrong query parameter names**  
   Conclusion: **Partial Pass**  
   Evidence:

- `docs/api-spec.md:256`
- `docs/api-spec.md:257`
- `repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go:27`
- `repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go:41`
- `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useDashboard.ts:38`
- `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useDashboard.ts:39`
  Impact:
- Static verification and client implementation against docs may fail due to contract mismatch.
  Minimum actionable fix:
- Align docs to `location_id` and `coach_id` everywhere for dashboard endpoint.

4. Severity: **Medium**  
   Title: **Immediate inline validation requirement is not clearly met in catalog form**  
   Conclusion: **Partial Pass**  
   Evidence:

- `repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx:59`
- `repo/frontend/src/routes/CatalogFormPage.tsx:60`
  Impact:
- Validation appears primarily submit-driven by default form mode, which may not satisfy explicit "immediate inline validation feedback" requirement.
  Minimum actionable fix:
- Use RHF validation mode `onChange` or `onBlur` with visible per-field feedback while editing.

5. Severity: **Medium**  
   Title: **Backup checksum is generated and stored, but verification workflow is not statically evidenced**

3	Minimal instructions to run the project locally with Docker.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Docker Desktop (with `docker compose`)
8	
9	## Startup
10	
11	From this `repo/` directory:
12	
13	```bash
14	docker compose up --build
15	```
16	
17	## Access
18	
19	- Frontend HTTPS: https://localhost:3443
20	- Frontend HTTP (redirects to HTTPS): http://localhost:3000
21	- Backend API (TLS): https://localhost:8080
22	- PostgreSQL: localhost:5432
23	
24	## Run Tests
25	
26	From this `repo/` directory:
27	
28	```bash
29	./run_tests.sh
30	```
31	
32	With coverage:
33	
34	```bash
35	./run_tests.sh --coverage
36	```
37	
38	## Stop
39	
40	```bash
41	docker compose down
42	```
43	
44	## Clean Reset (optional)
45	
46	Removes containers, networks, and volumes (deletes local DB data):
47	
48	```bash
49	docker compose down -v

64				existing.Description = policy.Description
65			}
66			existing.UpdatedAt = time.Now().UTC()
67			if err := s.retentionRepo.Update(ctx, existing); err != nil {
68				return fmt.Errorf("retention_service.Update: %w", err)
69			}
70			// Ensure policy.ID is set to the existing record's ID for the audit event.
71			policy.ID = existing.ID
72			policy.CreatedAt = existing.CreatedAt
73			policy.UpdatedAt = existing.UpdatedAt
74		}
75	
76		if err := s.audit.Log(ctx, "retention.policy.updated", "retention_policy", policy.ID, domain.SystemActorID, map[string]interface{}{
77			"entity_type":    policy.EntityType,
78			"retention_days": policy.RetentionDays,
79		}); err != nil {
80			slog.Default().Warn("audit log failed", "event", "retention.policy.updated", "error", err)
81		}
82	
83		return nil
84	}
85	
86	// purgeTargets maps entity_type values to the SQL table and timestamp column
87	// that determine record age for deletion. Only immutable/archivable records
88	// are eligible for hard deletion; core business entities are excluded.
89	var purgeTargets = map[string]struct{ table, column string }{
90		"sessions":            {table: "sessions", column: "absolute_expires_at"},
91		"captcha":             {table: "captcha_challenges", column: "created_at"},
92		"financial_records":   {table: "orders", column: "created_at"},
93		"procurement_records": {table: "purchase_orders", column: "created_at"},
94		"access_logs":         {table: "sessions", column: "absolute_expires_at"},
95	}
96	
97	// RunCleanup enforces each active retention policy by deleting records older
98	// than the configured retention window. Auditable purge events are recorded.
99	func (s *RetentionServiceImpl) RunCleanup(ctx context.Context) error {
100		policies, err := s.retentionRepo.List(ctx)
101		if err != nil {
102			return fmt.Errorf("retention_service.RunCleanup list: %w", err)
103		}
104	
105		now := time.Now().UTC()
106		for _, policy := range policies {
107			target, ok := purgeTargets[policy.EntityType]
108			if !ok {
109				slog.Default().Info("retention_service.RunCleanup: no purge target for entity type (skipped)",
110					"entity_type", policy.EntityType)
111				continue
112			}
113	
114			cutoff := now.AddDate(0, 0, -policy.RetentionDays)
115			selectQ := fmt.Sprintf("SELECT id FROM %s WHERE %s < $1", target.table, target.column)
116			rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx, selectQ, cutoff)
117			if err != nil {
118				slog.Default().Error("retention_service.RunCleanup: selection failed",
119					"entity_type", policy.EntityType, "error", err)
120				continue
121			}
122			var recordIDs []uuid.UUID
123			for rows.Next() {
124				var id uuid.UUID
125				if err := rows.Scan(&id); err != nil {
126					rows.Close()
127					slog.Default().Error("retention_service.RunCleanup: selection scan failed",
128						"entity_type", policy.EntityType, "error", err)
129					recordIDs = nil
130					break
131				}
132				recordIDs = append(recordIDs, id)
133			}
134			if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
135				slog.Default().Error("retention_service.RunCleanup: selection rows failed",
136					"entity_type", policy.EntityType, "error", err)
137				continue
138			}
139			rows.Close()
140			if len(recordIDs) == 0 {
141				continue
142			}
143	
144			for _, recordID := range recordIDs {
145				_ = s.audit.Log(ctx, "retention.record_deleted", target.table, recordID, domain.SystemActorID, map[string]interface{}{
146					"entity_type": policy.EntityType,
147					"cutoff":      cutoff.Format(time.RFC3339),
148				})
149			}
150	
151			deleteQ := fmt.Sprintf("DELETE FROM %s WHERE id = ANY($1)", target.table)
152			tag, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx, deleteQ, recordIDs)
153			if err != nil {
154				slog.Default().Error("retention_service.RunCleanup: purge failed",
155					"entity_type", policy.EntityType, "error", err)
156				continue
157			}
158	
159			_ = s.audit.Log(ctx, "retention.purged", "retention_policy", policy.ID, domain.SystemActorID, map[string]interface{}{
160				"entity_type":  policy.EntityType,
161				"cutoff":       cutoff.Format(time.RFC3339),
162				"rows_deleted": tag.RowsAffected(),
163				"record_ids":   recordIDs,
164			})
165		}
166		return nil
167	}
168	
169	// Compile-time interface assertion.
170	var _ RetentionService = (*RetentionServiceImpl)(nil)
171

1	package application
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"crypto/sha256"
6		"encoding/hex"
7		"fmt"
8		"io"
9		"log/slog"
10		"os"
11		"path/filepath"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/google/uuid"
15	
16		"fitcommerce/internal/domain"
17		"fitcommerce/internal/platform"
18		"fitcommerce/internal/security"
19		"fitcommerce/internal/store"
20	)
21	
22	// DumpFunc is a function that performs a database dump from connStr to destPath.
23	type DumpFunc func(ctx context.Context, connStr, destPath string) error
24	
25	// BackupServiceImpl implements BackupService.
26	type BackupServiceImpl struct {
27		backupRepo store.BackupRepository
28		cfg        platform.Config
29		dump       DumpFunc
30		audit      AuditService
31	}
32	
33	// NewBackupService creates a BackupServiceImpl with the given dependencies.
34	func NewBackupService(
35		backupRepo store.BackupRepository,
36		cfg platform.Config,
37		dump DumpFunc,
38		auditSvc AuditService,
39	) *BackupServiceImpl {
40		return &BackupServiceImpl{
41			backupRepo: backupRepo,
42			cfg:        cfg,
43			dump:       dump,
44			audit:      auditSvc,
45		}
46	}
47	
48	// Trigger initiates a new database backup run. performedBy is nil for scheduled/system
49	// runs and non-nil for manually triggered runs. The actor is recorded only in the audit
50	// event; it is not stored on the BackupRun record itself.
51	func (s *BackupServiceImpl) Trigger(ctx context.Context, performedBy *uuid.UUID) (*domain.BackupRun, error) {
52		run := &domain.BackupRun{
53			ID:        uuid.New(),
54			Status:    domain.BackupStatusRunning,
55			StartedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
56		}
57	
58		if err := s.backupRepo.Create(ctx, run); err != nil {
59			return nil, err
60		}
61	
62		destPath := filepath.Join(
63			s.cfg.BackupPath,
64			fmt.Sprintf("backup_%s.dump", run.StartedAt.Format("20060102_150405")),
65		)
66	
67		if err := os.MkdirAll(s.cfg.BackupPath, 0755); err != nil {
68			run.Status = domain.BackupStatusFailed
69			_ = s.backupRepo.Update(ctx, run) // best-effort; ignore error
70			return run, fmt.Errorf("backup_service.Trigger mkdir backup path: %w", err)
71		}
72	
73		if err := s.dump(ctx, s.cfg.DatabaseURL, destPath); err != nil {
74			run.Status = domain.BackupStatusFailed
75			_ = s.backupRepo.Update(ctx, run) // best-effort; ignore error
76			return run, fmt.Errorf("backup_service.Trigger dump: %w", err)
77		}
78	
79		// Encryption is required. Fail the backup if no key reference is configured so
80		// unencrypted archives are never silently stored.
81		if s.cfg.BackupEncryptionKeyRef == "" {
82			run.Status = domain.BackupStatusFailed
83			_ = s.backupRepo.Update(ctx, run)
84			return run, fmt.Errorf("backup_service.Trigger: FC_BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_KEY_REF is not set; backup encryption is mandatory")
85		}
86		key, err := security.DeriveKeyFromRef(s.cfg.BackupEncryptionKeyRef)
87		if err != nil {
88			run.Status = domain.BackupStatusFailed
89			_ = s.backupRepo.Update(ctx, run)
90			return run, err
91		}
92		if err := encryptFile(destPath, key); err != nil {
93			run.Status = domain.BackupStatusFailed
94			_ = s.backupRepo.Update(ctx, run)
95			return run, fmt.Errorf("backup_service.Trigger encrypt: %w", err)
96		}
97		run.EncryptionKeyRef = s.cfg.BackupEncryptionKeyRef
98	
99		// Persist metadata for the final encrypted archive rather than the plaintext dump.
100		if stat, err := os.Stat(destPath); err == nil {
101			run.FileSize = stat.Size()
102		}
103		checksum, err := sha256HexFile(destPath)
104		if err != nil {
105			run.Status = domain.BackupStatusFailed
106			_ = s.backupRepo.Update(ctx, run)
107			return run, fmt.Errorf("backup_service.Trigger checksum: %w", err)
108		}
109		run.Checksum = checksum
110		run.ChecksumAlgorithm = "sha256"
111	
112		now := time.Now().UTC()
113		run.Status = domain.BackupStatusCompleted
114		run.ArchivePath = destPath
115		run.CompletedAt = &now
116	
117		if err := s.backupRepo.Update(ctx, run); err != nil {
118			return run, fmt.Errorf("backup_service.Trigger update: %w", err)
119		}
120	
121		actorID := domain.SystemActorID
122		if performedBy != nil {
123			actorID = *performedBy
124		}
125	
126		if err := s.audit.Log(ctx, "backup.completed", "backup_run", run.ID, actorID, map[string]interface{}{
127			"path":      destPath,
128			"checksum":  checksum,
129			"file_size": run.FileSize,
130		}); err != nil {
131			slog.Default().Warn("audit log failed", "event", "backup.completed", "error", err)
132		}
133	
134		return run, nil
135	}
136	
137	// GetByID retrieves a backup run record by ID.
138	func (s *BackupServiceImpl) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id uuid.UUID) (*domain.BackupRun, error) {
139		return s.backupRepo.GetByID(ctx, id)
140	}
141	
142	// List returns a paginated list of backup run records.
143	func (s *BackupServiceImpl) List(ctx context.Context, page, pageSize int) ([]domain.BackupRun, int, error) {
144		return s.backupRepo.List(ctx, page, pageSize)
145	}
146	
147	// sha256HexFile streams the file at path through SHA-256 and returns the hex-encoded digest.
148	func sha256HexFile(path string) (string, error) {
149		f, err := os.Open(path)
150		if err != nil {
151			return "", err
152		}
153		defer f.Close()
154	
155		h := sha256.New()
156		if _, err := io.Copy(h, f); err != nil {
157			return "", err
158		}
159		return hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil)), nil
160	}
161	
162	// encryptFile reads the file at path, encrypts it with AES-GCM using key, and
163	// overwrites the file with the ciphertext.
164	func encryptFile(path string, key []byte) error {
165		plaintext, err := os.ReadFile(path)
166		if err != nil {
167			return err
168		}
169		ciphertext, err := security.EncryptAESGCM(plaintext, key)
170		if err != nil {
171			return err
172		}
173		return os.WriteFile(path, ciphertext, 0600)
174	}
175	
176	// Compile-time interface assertion.
177	var _ BackupService = (*BackupServiceImpl)(nil)
178

1	package security
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/aes"
5		"crypto/cipher"
6		"crypto/rand"
7		"crypto/sha256"
8		"fmt"
9		"io"
10	
11		"golang.org/x/crypto/hkdf"
12	)
13	
14	const aesNonceSize = 12 // GCM standard nonce size in bytes
15	
16	// GenerateAESKey returns a cryptographically random 32-byte key suitable for
17	// AES-256 encryption.
18	func GenerateAESKey() ([]byte, error) {
19		key := make([]byte, 32)
20		if _, err := rand.Read(key); err != nil {
21			return nil, fmt.Errorf("generating AES key: %w", err)
22		}
23		return key, nil
24	}
25	
26	// EncryptAESGCM encrypts plaintext using AES-256-GCM with a randomly generated
27	// nonce. The output is: nonce (12 bytes) ∥ ciphertext ∥ GCM tag.
28	func EncryptAESGCM(plaintext, key []byte) ([]byte, error) {
29		block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
30		if err != nil {
31			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating AES cipher: %w", err)
32		}
33	
34		gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
35		if err != nil {
36			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating GCM: %w", err)
37		}
38	
39		nonce := make([]byte, aesNonceSize)
40		if _, err = io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, nonce); err != nil {
41			return nil, fmt.Errorf("generating nonce: %w", err)
42		}
43	
44		ciphertext := gcm.Seal(nonce, nonce, plaintext, nil)
45		return ciphertext, nil
46	}
47	
48	// DecryptAESGCM decrypts data produced by EncryptAESGCM. The first 12 bytes of
49	// ciphertext are treated as the GCM nonce.
50	func DecryptAESGCM(ciphertext, key []byte) ([]byte, error) {
51		if len(ciphertext) < aesNonceSize {
52			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ciphertext too short")
53		}
54	
55		block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
56		if err != nil {
57			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating AES cipher: %w", err)
58		}
59	
60		gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
61		if err != nil {
62			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating GCM: %w", err)
63		}
64	
65		nonce := ciphertext[:aesNonceSize]
66		data := ciphertext[aesNonceSize:]
67	
68		plaintext, err := gcm.Open(nil, nonce, data, nil)
69		if err != nil {
70			return nil, fmt.Errorf("decrypting: %w", err)
71		}
72	
73		return plaintext, nil
74	}
75	
76	// DeriveKeyFromRef derives a 32-byte AES-256 key from the given key reference
77	// string using HKDF-SHA256. IKM = []byte(keyRef), salt = nil,
78	// info = "fitcommerce-biometric-v1". The derived key is deterministic for a
79	// given keyRef, so the caller must ensure keyRef is kept secret and rotated
80	// through the EncryptionKey rotation mechanism rather than changed ad-hoc.
81	func DeriveKeyFromRef(keyRef string) ([]byte, error) {
82		r := hkdf.New(sha256.New, []byte(keyRef), nil, []byte("fitcommerce-biometric-v1"))
83		key := make([]byte, 32)
84		if _, err := io.ReadFull(r, key); err != nil {
85			return nil, fmt.Errorf("DeriveKeyFromRef: %w", err)
86		}
87		return key, nil
88	}

4		"os"

6	)
7	
8	// Config holds all application configuration loaded from environment variables.
9	// All environment variables use the FC_ prefix.
10	type Config struct {
11		ServerPort                   string
12		DatabaseURL                  string
13		RunMigrationsOnStartup       bool
14		RunSeedOnStartup             bool
15		SessionIdleTimeoutMinutes    int
16		SessionAbsoluteTimeoutHours  int
17		LoginLockoutThreshold        int
18		LoginLockoutDurationMinutes  int
19		BackupPath                   string
20		BackupEncryptionKeyRef       string
21		ExportPath                   string
22		TLSCertFile                  string
23		TLSKeyFile                   string
24		AllowInsecureHTTP            bool
25		BiometricModuleEnabled       bool
26		BiometricKeyRotationDays     int
27		ClubTimezone                 string
28		LogLevel                     string
29	}
30	
31	// LoadConfig reads configuration from environment variables with FC_ prefix,
32	// applying defaults where appropriate.
33	func LoadConfig() Config {
34		return Config{
35			ServerPort:                   envOrDefault("FC_SERVER_PORT", "8080"),
36			DatabaseURL:                  envOrDefault("FC_DATABASE_URL", "postgres://fitcommerce:fitcommerce@localhost:5432/fitcommerce?sslmode=disable"),
37			RunMigrationsOnStartup:       envOrDefaultBool("FC_RUN_MIGRATIONS_ON_STARTUP", true),
38			RunSeedOnStartup:             envOrDefaultBool("FC_RUN_SEED_ON_STARTUP", true),
39			SessionIdleTimeoutMinutes:    envOrDefaultInt("FC_SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES", 30),
40			SessionAbsoluteTimeoutHours:  envOrDefaultInt("FC_SESSION_ABSOLUTE_TIMEOUT_HOURS", 12),
41			LoginLockoutThreshold:        envOrDefaultInt("FC_LOGIN_LOCKOUT_THRESHOLD", 5),
42			LoginLockoutDurationMinutes:  envOrDefaultInt("FC_LOGIN_LOCKOUT_DURATION_MINUTES", 15),
43			BackupPath:                   envOrDefault("FC_BACKUP_PATH", "/var/backups/fitcommerce"),
44			BackupEncryptionKeyRef:       envOrDefault("FC_BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_KEY_REF", ""),
45			ExportPath:                   envOrDefault("FC_EXPORT_PATH", "/tmp/fitcommerce-exports"),
46			TLSCertFile:                  envOrDefault("FC_TLS_CERT_FILE", ""),
47			TLSKeyFile:                   envOrDefault("FC_TLS_KEY_FILE", ""),
48			AllowInsecureHTTP:            envOrDefaultBool("FC_ALLOW_INSECURE_HTTP", false),
49			BiometricModuleEnabled:       envOrDefaultBool("FC_BIOMETRIC_MODULE_ENABLED", false),
50			BiometricKeyRotationDays:     envOrDefaultInt("FC_BIOMETRIC_KEY_ROTATION_DAYS", 90),
51			ClubTimezone:                 envOrDefault("FC_CLUB_TIMEZONE", "UTC"),
52			LogLevel:                     envOrDefault("FC_LOG_LEVEL", "info"),
53		}
54	}
55	
56	func envOrDefault(key, fallback string) string {
57		if val := os.Getenv(key); val != "" {
58			return val
59		}
60		return fallback
61	}
62	
63	func envOrDefaultInt(key string, fallback int) int {
64		val := os.Getenv(key)
65		if val == "" {
66			return fallback
67		}
68		parsed, err := strconv.Atoi(val)
69		if err != nil {
70			return fallback
71		}
72		return parsed
73	}
74	
75	func envOrDefaultBool(key string, fallback bool) bool {
76		val := os.Getenv(key)
77		if val == "" {
78			return fallback
79		}
80		parsed, err := strconv.ParseBool(val)
81		if err != nil {
82			return fallback
83		}
84		return parsed
85	}

1	package http

3	import (
4		"log/slog"
5	
6		"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
7		"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
8	
9		"fitcommerce/internal/security"
10	)
11	
12	// RegisterRoutes configures all API route groups on the given Echo instance.
13	func RegisterRoutes(
14		e *echo.Echo,
15		logger *slog.Logger,
16		pool *pgxpool.Pool,
17		authHandler *AuthHandler,
18		authMW echo.MiddlewareFunc,
19		itemH *ItemHandler,
20		inventoryH *InventoryHandler,
21		campaignH *CampaignHandler,
22		orderH *OrderHandler,
23		supplierH *SupplierHandler,
24		procurementH *ProcurementHandler,
25		varianceH *VarianceHandler,
26		reportH *ReportHandler,
27		backupH *BackupHandler,
28		retentionH *RetentionHandler,
29		biometricH *BiometricHandler,
30		userH *UserHandler,
31		adminH *AdminHandler,
32		dashboardH *DashboardHandler,
33		locationH *LocationHandler,
34		memberH *MemberHandler,
35		coachH *CoachHandler,
36		landedCostH *LandedCostHandler,
37	) {
38		api := e.Group("/api/v1")
39	
40		api.Use(RequestIDMiddleware())
41		api.Use(RecoverMiddleware(logger))
42	
43		registerAuthRoutes(api.Group("/auth"), authHandler, authMW)
44		registerDashboardRoutes(api.Group("/dashboard"), authMW, dashboardH)
45		registerItemRoutes(api.Group("/items"), authMW, itemH)
46		registerInventoryRoutes(api.Group("/inventory"), authMW, inventoryH)
47		registerWarehouseBinRoutes(api.Group("/warehouse-bins"), authMW, inventoryH)
48		registerCampaignRoutes(api.Group("/campaigns"), authMW, campaignH)
49		registerOrderRoutes(api.Group("/orders"), authMW, orderH)
50		registerSupplierRoutes(api.Group("/suppliers"), authMW, supplierH)
51		registerPurchaseOrderRoutes(api.Group("/purchase-orders"), authMW, procurementH)
52		registerVarianceRoutes(api.Group("/variances"), authMW, varianceH)
53		registerProcurementRoutes(api.Group("/procurement"), authMW, landedCostH)
54		registerReportRoutes(api.Group("/reports"), authMW, reportH)
55		registerExportRoutes(api.Group("/exports"), authMW, reportH)
56		registerAdminRoutes(api.Group("/admin"), authMW, backupH, retentionH, biometricH, userH, adminH)
57		registerLocationRoutes(api.Group("/locations"), authMW, locationH)
58		registerCoachRoutes(api.Group("/coaches"), authMW, coachH)
59		registerMemberRoutes(api.Group("/members"), authMW, memberH)
60	}
61	
62	func registerAuthRoutes(g *echo.Group, authHandler *AuthHandler, authMW echo.MiddlewareFunc) {
63		g.POST("/login", authHandler.Login)
64		g.POST("/logout", authHandler.Logout, authMW)
65		g.GET("/session", authHandler.GetSession, authMW)
66		g.POST("/captcha/verify", authHandler.VerifyCaptcha)
67	}
68	
69	func registerDashboardRoutes(g *echo.Group, authMW echo.MiddlewareFunc, h *DashboardHandler) {
70		viewDashboard := NewRequireRole(security.ActionViewDashboard)
71		g.Use(authMW)
72		g.GET("/kpis", h.GetKPIs, viewDashboard)
73	}
74	
75	func registerItemRoutes(g *echo.Group, authMW echo.MiddlewareFunc, h *ItemHandler) {
76		manageCatalog := NewRequireRole(security.ActionManageCatalog)
77		viewCatalog := NewRequireRole(security.ActionViewCatalog, security.ActionManageCatalog)
78	
79		g.Use(authMW)
80		g.POST("", h.CreateItem, manageCatalog)
81		g.GET("", h.ListItems, viewCatalog)
82		g.GET("/:id", h.GetItem, viewCatalog)
83		g.PUT("/:id", h.UpdateItem, manageCatalog)
84		g.POST("/:id/publish", h.PublishItem, manageCatalog)
85		g.POST("/:id/unpublish", h.UnpublishItem, manageCatalog)
86		g.POST("/batch-edit", h.BatchEdit, manageCatalog)
87	}
88	
89	func registerInventoryRoutes(g *echo.Group, authMW echo.MiddlewareFunc, h *InventoryHandler) {
90		manageInventory := NewRequireRole(security.ActionManageInventory)
91		// Inventory snapshots are operational data — restrict to roles that actively
92		// manage stock or procurement (Admin, OpsMgr, ProcurementSpecialist).
93		// ViewCatalog is intentionally excluded: it is granted to all roles including
94		// Coach and Member, who must not see raw stock-level data.
95		viewInventory := NewRequireRole(security.ActionManageInventory, security.ActionManageProcurement)
96	
97		g.Use(authMW)
98		g.GET("/snapshots", h.GetSnapshots, viewInventory)
99		g.POST("/adjustments", h.CreateAdjustment, manageInventory)
100		g.GET("/adjustments", h.ListAdjustments, manageInventory)
101	}

6		"github.com/google/uuid"

9	// BiometricEnrollment represents a user's biometric enrollment data,
10	// stored in encrypted form.
11	type BiometricEnrollment struct {
12		ID              uuid.UUID
13		UserID          uuid.UUID
14		EncryptedData   []byte
15		EncryptionKeyID uuid.UUID
16		CreatedAt       time.Time
17		UpdatedAt       time.Time
18	}
19	
20	// EncryptionKey represents a key used for encrypting biometric data.
21	type EncryptionKey struct {
22		ID           uuid.UUID
23		KeyReference string
24		Purpose      string // "biometric"
25		Status       EncryptionKeyStatus
26		ActivatedAt  time.Time
27		RotatedAt    *time.Time
28		ExpiresAt    time.Time
29	}
30	
31	// NeedsRotation returns true if the number of days since the key was activated
32	// meets or exceeds the specified rotation interval.
33	func (k *EncryptionKey) NeedsRotation(rotationDays int) bool {
34		daysSinceActivation := time.Since(k.ActivatedAt).Hours() / 24
35		return daysSinceActivation >= float64(rotationDays)
36	}
