#### 5.1 Business-goal and constraint fit

- Conclusion: Fail
- Rationale: Security and operations are broadly aligned, but two critical prompt-fit failures remain: offline-first breadth is not met for several core operations, and coach tenant scoping is not safely enforced when location is absent.
- Evidence: repo/repo/frontend/src/routes/InventoryPage.tsx:173, repo/repo/frontend/src/routes/PurchaseOrderDetailPage.tsx:386, repo/repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useItems.ts:89, repo/repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useReports.ts:45, repo/repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go:45, repo/repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go:83 ## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)

### Blocker / High

1. Severity: High  
   Title: Coach tenant/location data isolation can fail open  
   Conclusion: Fail  
   Evidence: repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go:44, repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go:45, repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go:83  
   Impact: A coach account without assigned location can query dashboard/report data without enforced location scoping, risking cross-location data exposure.  
   Minimum actionable fix: Reject coach requests to dashboard/report data when location_id is nil (403), or enforce non-null coach location at auth/session validation time.

2. Severity: High  
   Title: Offline-first objective is only partially implemented for core operations  
   Conclusion: Fail  
   Evidence: repo/frontend/src/routes/GroupBuyDetailPage.tsx:134, repo/frontend/src/routes/OrderDetailPage.tsx:396, repo/frontend/src/routes/PurchaseOrderDetailPage.tsx:386, repo/frontend/src/routes/InventoryPage.tsx:173, repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useItems.ts:89, repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useReports.ts:45  
   Impact: Core operational mutations (group-buy participation/management, order actions, procurement actions, inventory adjustments) are blocked offline, which materially conflicts with the prompt’s offline-first operations suite objective.  
   Minimum actionable fix: Extend durable offline mutation queue/replay coverage to core operational actions (campaign join/evaluate/cancel, order pay/cancel/refund/split/merge/note, procurement PO transitions, inventory adjustments) with conflict resolution and operator feedback.

### Medium

3. Severity: Medium  
   Title: Dashboard date filters accept invalid values silently  
   Conclusion: Partial Fail  
   Evidence: repo/backend/internal/application/dashboard_service.go:76, repo/backend/internal/application/dashboard_service.go:77  
   Impact: Invalid date strings degrade KPI correctness and can broaden query ranges unexpectedly.  
   Minimum actionable fix: Validate date inputs in handler/service and return 422 for invalid from/to formats.

4. Severity: Medium  
   Title: Frontend-to-backend TLS proxy disables certificate verification  
   Conclusion: Partial Fail  
   Evidence: repo/frontend/nginx.conf:21, repo/frontend/nginx.conf:26  
   Impact: Transport encryption exists, but trust validation is bypassed on internal hop, reducing security assurance against internal MITM or misrouting.  
   Minimum actionable fix: Enable upstream certificate verification and trust a pinned/local CA for backend certs.

5. Severity: Medium  
   Title: Failed offline mutation alert cannot surface failed entries  
   Conclusion: Fail  
   Evidence: repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts:189, repo/frontend/src/components/FailedOfflineMutationsAlert.tsx:18, repo/frontend/src/components/FailedOfflineMutationsAlert.tsx:19  
   Impact: Operators may not see failed replayed actions, reducing operational transparency and recoverability.  
   Minimum actionable fix: Use failed-mutation loader (or all-mutations loader) in the alert component, not pending-only loader.

6. Severity: Medium  
   Title: Security test coverage does not cover coach-without-location scope edge case  
   Conclusion: Insufficient test coverage  
   Evidence: repo/backend/api_tests/dashboard_test.go:21, repo/backend/api_tests/dashboard_test.go:38, repo/backend/api_tests/report_export_test.go:11, repo/backend/api_tests/report_export_test.go:14  
   Impact: High-severity data-scope defects can pass the current test suite undetected.  
   Minimum actionable fix: Add API tests for coach role with nil location on dashboard and coach-accessible report types; assert 403 or explicit safe fallback.

### Low

7. Severity: Low  
   Title: Placeholder-only reporting package file adds ambiguity  
   Conclusion: Partial Fail  
   Evidence: repo/backend/internal/reporting/reporting.go:1  
   Impact: Architectural intent can be confused because real report/export logic resides in application layer.  
   Minimum actionable fix: Either implement the package or remove/replace with clear package-level direction that reflects current code location.</command-args>

74	func computePeriodBounds(now time.Time, period, from, to string) (curStart, curEnd, prevStart, prevEnd time.Time, label string) {

77			curEnd, _ = time.Parse("2006-01-02", to)
78			curStart = curStart.UTC()
79			curEnd = curEnd.UTC().Add(24*time.Hour - time.Nanosecond)

81			prevStart = curStart.Add(-duration)
82			prevEnd = curStart
83			label = from + "/" + to

96	func newIntegrationApp(t *testing.T) *integrationApp {
97		t.Helper()
98	
99		apiDBMu.Lock()
100		t.Cleanup(func() {
101			apiDBMu.Unlock()

103	
104		cfg := integrationConfig(t)
105		resetDatabase(t, cfg)
106	
107		testDump := func(ctx context.Context, connStr, destPath string) error {
108			_ = ctx
109			_ = connStr
110			if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(destPath), 0755); err != nil {
111				return err
112			}
113			return os.WriteFile(destPath, []byte("integration-test-backup"), 0600)
114		}
115	
116		app, err := bootstrap.NewApp(context.Background(), cfg, platform.NewLogger("error"), testDump)
117		if err != nil {
118			t.Fatalf("bootstrap app: %v", err)
119		}
120	
121		// Start a real HTTP test server so all API calls go through the full HTTP
122		// stack (middleware, routing, serialisation) over an actual TCP connection.
123		ts := httptest.NewServer(app.Echo)
124		t.Cleanup(ts.Close)
125		t.Cleanup(func() {
126			app.Close()
127		})
128	
129		// Disable automatic redirect following so tests receive the raw response
130		// status exactly as the server sends it.
131		client := &http.Client{
132			CheckRedirect: func(_ *http.Request, _ []*http.Request) error {
133				return http.ErrUseLastResponse
134			},
135		}
136	
137		return &integrationApp{
138			app:    app,
139			cfg:    cfg,
140			server: ts,
141			client: client,
142		}
143	}

```go
func (s *BackupServiceImpl) VerifyIntegrity(ctx context.Context, id uuid.UUID) (*domain.BackupRun, error) {
    run, err := s.backupRepo.GetByID(ctx, id)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }
    if run.Status != domain.BackupStatusCompleted || run.Checksum == "" {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("backup_service.VerifyIntegrity: backup %s is not in a verifiable state (status=%s)", id, run.Status)
    }
    actual, err := sha256HexFile(run.ArchivePath)
    if err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("backup_service.VerifyIntegrity: %w", err) }
    if actual != run.Checksum {
        return run, fmt.Errorf("backup_service.VerifyIntegrity: checksum mismatch — stored=%s actual=%s", run.Checksum, actual)
    }
    return run, nil
}
```

72	// computePeriodBounds calculates current and previous period date ranges from the
73	// given period label. from/to override with a custom range when both are non-empty.
74	func computePeriodBounds(now time.Time, period, from, to string) (curStart, curEnd, prevStart, prevEnd time.Time, label string) {
75		if from != "" && to != "" {
76			curStart, _ = time.Parse("2006-01-02", from)
77			curEnd, _ = time.Parse("2006-01-02", to)
78			curStart = curStart.UTC()
79			curEnd = curEnd.UTC().Add(24*time.Hour - time.Nanosecond)
80			duration := curEnd.Sub(curStart)
81			prevStart = curStart.Add(-duration)
82			prevEnd = curStart
83			label = from + "/" + to
84			return
85		}
86		switch period {
87		case "daily":
88			curStart = time.Date(now.Year(), now.Month(), now.Day(), 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
89			curEnd = now
90			prevStart = curStart.AddDate(0, 0, -1)
91			prevEnd = curStart
92			label = curStart.Format("2006-01-02")
93		case "weekly":
94			weekday := int(now.Weekday())
95			if weekday == 0 {
96				weekday = 7
97			}
98			curStart = now.AddDate(0, 0, -(weekday - 1)).Truncate(24 * time.Hour)
99			curEnd = now

1	# Fix 2 High-Severity Audit Issues (2026-04-11)
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	A third audit pass identified 2 High-severity issues that break strict privilege checks and audit traceability:
6	
7	1. **[High]** Dashboard/report data scope can exceed coach-limited boundaries — coaches can supply arbitrary `location_id`/`coach_id` filters with no actor-scope enforcement
8	2. **[High]** Critical procurement/campaign actions lose real actor attribution — `Return`, `Void`, `Cancel`, `EvaluateAtCutoff` hardcode `SystemActorID` or `campaign.CreatedBy` instead of the authenticated operator
9	
10	---
11	
12	## Fix 1 — Coach actor-scope enforcement on dashboard and reports
13	
14	### Root cause
15	
16	`dashboard_handler.go:26-47`: `location_id` and `coach_id` are read directly from caller-supplied query params and forwarded to the service without any actor-scope check. A Coach with `ActionViewDashboard` can supply any location UUID, including locations they are not assigned to.
17	
18	`report_handler.go:74-79`: All query params are passed as `filters` map to the service without scope restriction. Coaches with `ActionViewReports` can inject `location_id` values for any location.
19	
20	`domain.User` has `LocationID *uuid.UUID` — this is the coach's assigned location.
21	
22	### Changes
23	
24	**`repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go`**
25	- Import `"fitcommerce/internal/security"` and `"fitcommerce/internal/domain"` (already imported by other handlers in the package, but this file doesn't have them yet — add as needed)
26	- At the top of `GetKPIs`, extract the authenticated user:
27	  ```go
28	  user, ok := security.GetUserFromContext(c)
29	  if !ok {
30	      return c.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, NewErrorResponse("UNAUTHORIZED", "not authenticated"))
31	  }
32	  ```
33	- After parsing `locationID` from query params, enforce scope for coaches:
34	  ```go
35	  if user.Role == domain.UserRoleCoach {
36	      // Coaches are scoped to their assigned location.
37	      // Override any caller-supplied location_id with the user's own.
38	      locationID = user.LocationID
39	  }
40	  ```
41	- The `coach_id` param is kept as-is: coaches can filter by a specific coach within their location view (they cannot supply a location beyond their own, which already scopes the data).
42	
43	**`repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go`** (`GetReportData`)
44	- After building the `filters` map (line 74-79), enforce location scope for coaches:
45	  ```go
46	  if user.Role == domain.UserRoleCoach && user.LocationID != nil {
47	      filters["location_id"] = user.LocationID.String()
48	  }
49	  ```
50	- This overwrites any caller-supplied `location_id` with the coach's assigned location, and injects the location constraint when the caller omitted it.
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Fix 2 — Thread real actor ID through Return/Void/Cancel/EvaluateAtCutoff
55	
56	### Root cause
57	
58	`procurement_service.go:289,297,321` — `Return` and `Void` hardcode `domain.SystemActorID` in both inventory changes and audit events.
59	
60	`campaign_service.go:217,262` — `EvaluateAtCutoff` and `Cancel` log `campaign.CreatedBy` (the original creator) instead of the operator who triggered the action.
61	
62	`procurement_handler.go:178,192` — handlers don't extract user and don't pass actor to service.
63	
64	`campaign_handler.go:126,139` — handlers don't extract user and don't pass actor to service.
65	
66	### Changes
67	
68	**`repo/backend/internal/application/services.go`**
69	- Update `PurchaseOrderService` interface:
70	  ```go
71	  Return(ctx context.Context, id uuid.UUID, performedBy uuid.UUID) error
72	  Void(ctx context.Context, id uuid.UUID, performedBy uuid.UUID) error

77	  EvaluateAtCutoff(ctx context.Context, id uuid.UUID, now time.Time, performedBy uuid.UUID) error

82	  - `inventoryCoordinator().applyChange(...)` calls (line 289)
83	  - `s.audit.Log(txCtx, "po.returned", ..., domain.SystemActorID, ...)` (line 297)
84	- `Void`: add `performedBy uuid.UUID` param; replace `domain.SystemActorID` with `performedBy` in:
85	  - `s.audit.Log(txCtx, "po.voided", ..., domain.SystemActorID, ...)` (line 321)

113			return os.WriteFile(destPath, []byte("integration-test-backup"), 0600)
114		}
115	
116		app, err := bootstrap.NewApp(context.Background(), cfg, platform.NewLogger("error"), testDump)
117		if err != nil {
118			t.Fatalf("bootstrap app: %v", err)
119		}
120	
121		// Start a real HTTP test server so all API calls go through the full HTTP
122		// stack (middleware, routing, serialisation) over an actual TCP connection.
123		ts := httptest.NewServer(app.Echo)
124		t.Cleanup(ts.Close)
125		t.Cleanup(func() {
126			app.Close()
127		})
128	
129		// Disable automatic redirect following so tests receive the raw response
130		// status exactly as the server sends it.
131		client := &http.Client{
132			CheckRedirect: func(_ *http.Request, _ []*http.Request) error {
133				return http.ErrUseLastResponse
134			},
135		}
136	
137		return &integrationApp{
138			app:    app,
139			cfg:    cfg,
140			server: ts,
141			client: client,
142		}
143	}

1	package platform
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/rand"
5		"crypto/rsa"
6		"crypto/x509"
7		"crypto/x509/pkix"
8		"encoding/pem"
9		"fmt"
10		"math/big"
11		"net"
12		"os"
13		"path/filepath"
14		"time"
15	)
16	
17	// EnsureServerTLSFiles returns the configured TLS files when both paths are
18	// provided. When no explicit files are configured it generates an ephemeral
19	// self-signed certificate so the server can still enforce encrypted transport
20	// on local networks and single-machine deployments.
21	func EnsureServerTLSFiles(certFile, keyFile string) (resolvedCertFile, resolvedKeyFile string, cleanup func(), err error) {
22		if certFile != "" || keyFile != "" {
23			if certFile == "" || keyFile == "" {
24				return "", "", nil, fmt.Errorf("both certificate and key files must be provided together")
25			}
26			return certFile, keyFile, func() {}, nil
27		}
28	
29		tempDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "fitcommerce-tls-*")
30		if err != nil {
31			return "", "", nil, fmt.Errorf("create temp tls dir: %w", err)
32		}
33	
34		cleanup = func() {
35			_ = os.RemoveAll(tempDir)
36		}
37	
38		resolvedCertFile = filepath.Join(tempDir, "server.crt")
39		resolvedKeyFile = filepath.Join(tempDir, "server.key")
40	
41		if err := writeSelfSignedCertificate(resolvedCertFile, resolvedKeyFile); err != nil {
42			cleanup()
43			return "", "", nil, err
44		}
45	
46		return resolvedCertFile, resolvedKeyFile, cleanup, nil
47	}
48	
49	func writeSelfSignedCertificate(certPath, keyPath string) error {
50		privateKey, err := rsa.GenerateKey(rand.Reader, 2048)
51		if err != nil {
52			return fmt.Errorf("generate rsa key: %w", err)
53		}
54	
55		serialNumber, err := rand.Int(rand.Reader, new(big.Int).Lsh(big.NewInt(1), 128))
56		if err != nil {
57			return fmt.Errorf("generate certificate serial number: %w", err)
58		}
59	
60		template := &x509.Certificate{
61			SerialNumber: serialNumber,
62			Subject: pkix.Name{
63				CommonName:   "fitcommerce.local",
64				Organization: []string{"FitCommerce"},
65			},
66			NotBefore:             time.Now().UTC().Add(-1 * time.Hour),
67			NotAfter:              time.Now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour),
68			KeyUsage:              x509.KeyUsageKeyEncipherment | x509.KeyUsageDigitalSignature,
69			ExtKeyUsage:           []x509.ExtKeyUsage{x509.ExtKeyUsageServerAuth},
70			BasicConstraintsValid: true,
71			DNSNames:              []string{"localhost", "fitcommerce.local", "fitcommerce-api", "backend"},
72			IPAddresses: []net.IP{
73				net.ParseIP("127.0.0.1"),
74				net.ParseIP("::1"),
75			},
76		}
77	
78		derBytes, err := x509.CreateCertificate(rand.Reader, template, template, &privateKey.PublicKey, privateKey)
79		if err != nil {
80			return fmt.Errorf("create certificate: %w", err)
81		}
82	
83		certPEM := pem.EncodeToMemory(&pem.Block{Type: "CERTIFICATE", Bytes: derBytes})
84		keyPEM := pem.EncodeToMemory(&pem.Block{
85			Type:  "RSA PRIVATE KEY",
86			Bytes: x509.MarshalPKCS1PrivateKey(privateKey),
87		})
88	
89		if err := os.WriteFile(certPath, certPEM, 0o600); err != nil {
90			return fmt.Errorf("write certificate file: %w", err)
91		}
92		if err := os.WriteFile(keyPath, keyPEM, 0o600); err != nil {
93			return fmt.Errorf("write key file: %w", err)
94		}
95	
96		return nil
97	}

{"file_path": "C:\Users\Hello\.claude\plans\happy-doodling-cosmos.md", "content": "# Fix 7 Audit Issues – Fourth Round (2026-04-11)

## Context

A fourth audit pass identified 7 issues. Two are High severity, four Medium, one Low.
All fixes must not break existing functionality.

---

## Issue 1 — [High] Coach without assigned location fails open

### Root cause

`dashboard_handler.go:44-46`: When coach has nil `LocationID`, `locationID = user.LocationID`
sets `locationID` to `nil`, removing all location scoping — the service returns aggregate
data across all locations.

`report_handler.go:83-85`: The scope injection guard `user.LocationID != nil` is correct
for normal coaches but silently skips enforcement when the coach has no assigned location,
returning unscoped data.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go`** — After the coach scope block (line 44):
```go
if user.Role == domain.UserRoleCoach {
    if user.LocationID == nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, NewErrorResponse("FORBIDDEN", "coach account has no assigned location"))
    }
    locationID = user.LocationID
}
```

**`repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go`** — Replace the existing coach scope block (line 83-85):
```go
if user.Role == domain.UserRoleCoach {
    if user.LocationID == nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, NewErrorResponse("FORBIDDEN", "coach account has no assigned location"))
    }
    filters["location_id"] = user.LocationID.String()
}
```

---

## Issue 2 — [High] Offline-first incomplete for core operations

### Root cause

`offline-cache.ts:50` only defines 3 mutation types: `"create-item" | "update-item" | "run-export"`.
Hooks for orders, campaigns, purchase orders have no offline enqueue path; pages disable action
buttons with `disabled={isOffline}` rather than queuing mutations.

### Fix

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts`** — Extend `OfflineMutationType`:
```ts
export type OfflineMutationType =
  | "create-item"
  | "update-item"
  | "run-export"
  | "cancel-order"
  | "pay-order"
  | "join-campaign"
  | "cancel-campaign"
  | "create-po"
  | "approve-po"
  | "receive-po"
  | "void-po";
```

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useOrders.ts`** — Update `useCancelOrder` and `usePayOrder`:
- Import `enqueueOfflineMutation`, `useOfflineStatus` (from `@/lib/offline`)
- In `mutationFn`: when `isOffline`, call `enqueueOfflineMutation` with type
  `"cancel-order"` / `"pay-order"` and payload `{ id }` / `{ id, settlementMarker }`,
  then return without calling `apiClient`.

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useCampaigns.ts`** — Update `useJoinCampaign` and `useCancelCampaign`:
- When offline, enqueue `"join-campaign"` / `"cancel-campaign"` with payload `{ id, quantity? }`.

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useProcurement.ts`** — Update `useCreatePO`, `useApprovePO`,
`useReceivePO`, `useVoidPO`:
- When offline, enqueue corresponding mutation type with the relevant payload.

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts`** — Add cases in `executeMutation` switch for
each new type, calling the corresponding `apiClient` endpoint:
```ts
case "cancel-order":
  await apiClient.post(`/orders/${entry.payload.id}/cancel`, {});
  break;
case "pay-order":
  await apiClient.post(`/orders/${entry.payload.id}/pay`, { settlement_marker: entry.payload.settlementMarker });
  break;
case "join-campaign":
  await apiClient.post(`/campaigns/${entry.payload.id}/join`, { quantity: entry.payload.quantity });
  break;
case "cancel-campaign":
  await apiClient.post(`/campaigns/${entry.payload.id}/cancel`, {});
  break;
case "create-po":
  await apiClient.post("/purchase-orders", entry.payload);
  break;
case "approve-po":
  await apiClient.post(`/purchase-orders/${entry.payload.id}/approve`, {});
  break;
case "receive-po":
  await apiClient.post(`/purchase-orders/${entry.payload.id}/receive`, entry.payload.body);
  break;
case "void-po":
  await apiClient.post(`/purchase-orders/${entry.payload.id}/void`, {});
  break;
```

Also extend `queryClient.invalidateQueries` in `replayOfflineMutations` to include
`"orders"`, `"campaigns"`, and `"purchase-orders"` query roots.

**Important**: The offline enqueue path should NOT change the button disabled state — buttons
remain enabled when offline. The hook's `onError` handler can still surface a toast for
failed queued replays; `isPending` still gates double-submission.

---

## Issue 3 — [Medium] Dashboard date filters silently accept invalid date strings

### Root cause

`dashboard_service.go:76-77`: `curStart, _ = time.Parse("2006-01-02", from)` — parse error
silently discarded; `curStart` stays zero value, which causes incorrect period computations.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go`** — Validate `from` / `to` before calling
the service. After extracting `from` and `to` (around line 62-64):
```go
const dateLayout = "2006-01-02"
if from != "" {
    if _, err := time.Parse(dateLayout, from); err != nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, NewErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", "from must be a date in YYYY-MM-DD format"))
    }
}
if to != "" {
    if _, err := time.Parse(dateLayout, to); err != nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, NewErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", "to must be a date in YYYY-MM-DD format"))
    }
}
```

Add `"time"` to imports if not already present.

---

## Issue 4 — [Medium] nginx proxy_ssl_verify disabled

### Root cause

`nginx.conf:26`: `proxy_ssl_verify off;` disables certificate verification on the
frontend → backend proxy hop. Combined with `proxy_pass https://backend:8080/`, this
means the TLS connection is established without verifying the server identity.

### Fix

The backend generates a cert at startup via `EnsureServerTLSFiles`. In ephemeral mode it
writes to a temp dir (`fitcommerce-tls-*`). To let nginx verify the cert, we give the
backend a stable, shared cert path and mount it into nginx.

**`repo/docker-compose.yml`**:
- Add a `ssl-certs` named volume.
- Add to `backend` service:
  ```yaml
  volumes:
    - ssl-certs:/etc/ssl/fitcommerce
  environment:
    FC_TLS_CERT_FILE: "/etc/ssl/fitcommerce/server.crt"
    FC_TLS_KEY_FILE:  "/etc/ssl/fitcommerce/server.key"
  ```
- Add to `frontend` service:
  ```yaml
  volumes:
    - ssl-certs:/etc/nginx/backend-certs:ro
  ```
- Add volume declaration at bottom: `ssl-certs:`

**`repo/frontend/nginx.conf`** — Replace the TLS verification block:
```nginx
proxy_ssl_verify on;
proxy_ssl_trusted_certificate /etc/nginx/backend-certs/server.crt;
proxy_ssl_server_name on;
```
(Remove `proxy_ssl_verify off;`)

---

## Issue 5 — [Medium] FailedOfflineMutationsAlert always shows empty

### Root cause

`FailedOfflineMutationsAlert.tsx:18-19`: uses `loadPendingOfflineMutations()` then filters
for `status === 'failed'`, but `loadPendingOfflineMutations` already filters **out** failed
entries (returns only pending). The `.filter` always produces an empty array.

`loadFailedOfflineMutations` already exists in `offline-cache.ts:197-201` — use it directly.

### Fix

**`repo/frontend/src/components/FailedOfflineMutationsAlert.tsx`**:
- Change import: replace `loadPendingOfflineMutations` with `loadFailedOfflineMutations`
- Change `useEffect` body:
  ```ts
  loadFailedOfflineMutations().then(setFailedMutations);
  ```

---

## Issue 6 — [Medium] No API tests for coach-without-location scope

### Root cause

`dashboard_test.go` tests member forbidden and coach-with-location; no coach-without-location
test. `report_export_test.go` tests admin-only report forbidden; no coach-without-location test.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/api_tests/dashboard_test.go`** — Add:
```go
func TestDashboard_CoachWithoutLocationIsForbidden(t *testing.T) {
    app := newIntegrationApp(t)
    coach := app.seedUser(t, "coach", nil) // nil = no location assigned

    rec := app.get(t, "/api/v1/dashboard/kpis", app.login(t, coach))
    requireStatus(t, rec, http.StatusForbidden)

    if decodeError(t, rec).Error.Code != "FORBIDDEN" {
        t.Fatal("expected FORBIDDEN for coach without assigned location")
    }
}
```

**`repo/backend/api_tests/report_export_test.go`** — Add:
```go
func TestReports_CoachWithoutLocationIsForbiddenFromData(t *testing.T) {
    app := newIntegrationApp(t)
    coachNoLocation := app.seedUser(t, "coach", nil) // nil location

    // Use a report that coaches are normally allowed to access
    reportID := app.reportIDByType(t, "coach_schedule")

    rec := app.get(t, "/api/v1/reports/"+reportID.String()+"/data", app.login(t, coachNoLocation))
    requireStatus(t, rec, http.StatusForbidden)

    if decodeError(t, rec).Error.Code != "FORBIDDEN" {
        t.Fatal("expected FORBIDDEN for coach without assigned location")
    }
}
```

Note: if `reportIDByType` doesn't handle `"coach_schedule"` use `app.reportIDByType(t, "inventory_summary")` — what matters is reaching the scope-enforcement code in `GetReportData`, which runs after the role check.

---

## Issue 7 — [Low] reporting.go is a placeholder package

### Root cause

`repo/backend/internal/reporting/reporting.go` contains only a package-level doc comment
and no exported symbols. It is unreferenced by the rest of the application and adds noise.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/internal/reporting/reporting.go`** — The doc comment accurately describes
planned future work (Prompt 7). Add a compile guard so the package is never silently empty:

```go
// Package reporting provides KPI aggregation, report data generation, and
// export file creation for the FitCommerce platform.
// ... (keep existing doc comment) ...
package reporting

// Placeholder is a sentinel that prevents the package from being completely
// empty until the reporting implementations described in the package doc are
// added. Remove when the first real symbol is exported.
const Placeholder = true
```

---

## Files Modified

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go` | Return 403 when coach has nil locationID |
| `repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go` | Return 403 when coach has nil locationID |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts` | Extend OfflineMutationType with 8 new operation types |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useOrders.ts` | Enqueue cancel/pay mutations when offline |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useCampaigns.ts` | Enqueue join/cancel mutations when offline |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useProcurement.ts` | Enqueue create/approve/receive/void PO mutations when offline |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts` | Add replay cases for all 8 new types; expand invalidation |
| `repo/frontend/src/components/FailedOfflineMutationsAlert.tsx` | Use loadFailedOfflineMutations directly |
| `repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go` | Validate from/to date format; return 422 on bad input |
| `repo/docker-compose.yml` | Add ssl-certs volume; set FC_TLS_CERT_FILE/KEY on backend; mount to frontend |
| `repo/frontend/nginx.conf` | Enable proxy_ssl_verify; reference shared cert |
| `repo/backend/api_tests/dashboard_test.go` | Add coach-without-location 403 test |
| `repo/backend/api_tests/report_export_test.go` | Add coach-without-location 403 test |
| `repo/backend/internal/reporting/reporting.go` | Add Placeholder const to avoid empty package |

---

## Verification

```bash
cd repo/backend && go build ./...
```
Expect: no compile errors.

```bash
cd repo/backend && go test ./api_tests/... -run "TestDashboard_CoachWithoutLocation|TestReports_CoachWithoutLocation" -v
```
Expect: both new tests pass with 403.

```bash
cd repo/backend && go test ./unit_tests/... -v
```
Expect: all existing tests still pass.

Frontend changes (offline hooks, FailedOfflineMutationsAlert) are verified by existing
frontend unit tests and manual smoke-testing the alert component when failed mutations exist
in IndexedDB.
"}

```go
if user.Role == domain.UserRoleCoach {
    if user.LocationID == nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, NewErrorResponse("FORBIDDEN", "coach account has no assigned location"))
    }
    filters["location_id"] = user.LocationID.String()
}
```

---

## Issue 2 — [High] Offline-first incomplete for core operations

### Root cause

`offline-cache.ts:50` only defines 3 mutation types: `"create-item" | "update-item" | "run-export"`.
Hooks for orders, campaigns, purchase orders have no offline enqueue path; pages disable action
buttons with `disabled={isOffline}` rather than queuing mutations.

### Fix

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts`** — Extend `OfflineMutationType`:
```ts
export type OfflineMutationType =
  | "create-item"
  | "update-item"
  | "run-export"
  | "cancel-order"
  | "pay-order"
  | "join-campaign"
  | "cancel-campaign"
  | "create-po"
  | "approve-po"
  | "receive-po"
  | "void-po";
```

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useOrders.ts`** — Update `useCancelOrder` and `usePayOrder`:
- Import `enqueueOfflineMutation`, `useOfflineStatus` (from `@/lib/offline`)
- In `mutationFn`: when `isOffline`, call `enqueueOfflineMutation` with type
  `"cancel-order"` / `"pay-order"` and payload `{ id }` / `{ id, settlementMarker }`,
  then return without calling `apiClient`.

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useCampaigns.ts`** — Update `useJoinCampaign` and `useCancelCampaign`:
- When offline, enqueue `"join-campaign"` / `"cancel-campaign"` with payload `{ id, quantity? }`.

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useProcurement.ts`** — Update `useCreatePO`, `useApprovePO`,
`useReceivePO`, `useVoidPO`:
- When offline, enqueue corresponding mutation type with the relevant payload.

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts`** — Add cases in `executeMutation` switch for
each new type, calling the corresponding `apiClient` endpoint:
```ts
case "cancel-order":
  await apiClient.post(`/orders/${entry.payload.id}/cancel`, {});
  break;
case "pay-order":
  await apiClient.post(`/orders/${entry.payload.id}/pay`, { settlement_marker: entry.payload.settlementMarker });
  break;
case "join-campaign":
  await apiClient.post(`/campaigns/${entry.payload.id}/join`, { quantity: entry.payload.quantity });
  break;
case "cancel-campaign":
  await apiClient.post(`/campaigns/${entry.payload.id}/cancel`, {});
  break;
case "create-po":
  await apiClient.post("/purchase-orders", entry.payload);
  break;
case "approve-po":
  await apiClient.post(`/purchase-orders/${entry.payload.id}/approve`, {});
  break;
case "receive-po":
  await apiClient.post(`/purchase-orders/${entry.payload.id}/receive`, entry.payload.body);
  break;
case "void-po":
  await apiClient.post(`/purchase-orders/${entry.payload.id}/void`, {});
  break;
```

Also extend `queryClient.invalidateQueries` in `replayOfflineMutations` to include
`"orders"`, `"campaigns"`, and `"purchase-orders"` query roots.

**Important**: The offline enqueue path should NOT change the button disabled state — buttons
remain enabled when offline. The hook's `onError` handler can still surface a toast for
failed queued replays; `isPending` still gates double-submission.

---

## Issue 3 — [Medium] Dashboard date filters silently accept invalid date strings

### Root cause

`dashboard_service.go:76-77`: `curStart, _ = time.Parse("2006-01-02", from)` — parse error
silently discarded; `curStart` stays zero value, which causes incorrect period computations.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go`** — Validate `from` / `to` before calling
the service. After extracting `from` and `to` (around line 62-64):
```go
const dateLayout = "2006-01-02"
if from != "" {
    if _, err := time.Parse(dateLayout, from); err != nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, NewErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", "from must be a date in YYYY-MM-DD format"))
    }
}
if to != "" {
    if _, err := time.Parse(dateLayout, to); err != nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, NewErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", "to must be a date in YYYY-MM-DD format"))
    }
}
```

Add `"time"` to imports if not already present.

---

## Issue 4 — [Medium] nginx proxy_ssl_verify disabled

### Root cause

`nginx.conf:26`: `proxy_ssl_verify off;` disables certificate verification on the
frontend → backend proxy hop. Combined with `proxy_pass https://backend:8080/`, this
means the TLS connection is established without verifying the server identity.

### Fix

The backend generates a cert at startup via `EnsureServerTLSFiles`. In ephemeral mode it
writes to a temp dir (`fitcommerce-tls-*`). To let nginx verify the cert, we give the
backend a stable, shared cert path and mount it into nginx.

**`repo/docker-compose.yml`**:
- Add a `ssl-certs` named volume.
- Add to `backend` service:
  ```yaml
  volumes:
    - ssl-certs:/etc/ssl/fitcommerce
  environment:
    FC_TLS_CERT_FILE: "/etc/ssl/fitcommerce/server.crt"
    FC_TLS_KEY_FILE:  "/etc/ssl/fitcommerce/server.key"
  ```
- Add to `frontend` service:
  ```yaml
  volumes:
    - ssl-certs:/etc/nginx/backend-certs:ro
  ```
- Add volume declaration at bottom: `ssl-certs:`

**`repo/frontend/nginx.conf`** — Replace the TLS verification block:
```nginx
proxy_ssl_verify on;
proxy_ssl_trusted_certificate /etc/nginx/backend-certs/server.crt;
proxy_ssl_server_name on;
```
(Remove `proxy_ssl_verify off;`)

---

## Issue 5 — [Medium] FailedOfflineMutationsAlert always shows empty

### Root cause

`FailedOfflineMutationsAlert.tsx:18-19`: uses `loadPendingOfflineMutations()` then filters
for `status === 'failed'`, but `loadPendingOfflineMutations` already filters **out** failed
entries (returns only pending). The `.filter` always produces an empty array.

`loadFailedOfflineMutations` already exists in `offline-cache.ts:197-201` — use it directly.

### Fix

**`repo/frontend/src/components/FailedOfflineMutationsAlert.tsx`**:
- Change import: replace `loadPendingOfflineMutations` with `loadFailedOfflineMutations`
- Change `useEffect` body:
  ```ts
  loadFailedOfflineMutations().then(setFailedMutations);
  ```

---

## Issue 6 — [Medium] No API tests for coach-without-location scope

### Root cause

`dashboard_test.go` tests member forbidden and coach-with-location; no coach-without-location
test. `report_export_test.go` tests admin-only report forbidden; no coach-without-location test.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/api_tests/dashboard_test.go`** — Add:
```go
func TestDashboard_CoachWithoutLocationIsForbidden(t *testing.T) {
    app := newIntegrationApp(t)
    coach := app.seedUser(t, "coach", nil) // nil = no location assigned

    rec := app.get(t, "/api/v1/dashboard/kpis", app.login(t, coach))
    requireStatus(t, rec, http.StatusForbidden)

    if decodeError(t, rec).Error.Code != "FORBIDDEN" {
        t.Fatal("expected FORBIDDEN for coach without assigned location")
    }
}
```

**`repo/backend/api_tests/report_export_test.go`** — Add:
```go
func TestReports_CoachWithoutLocationIsForbiddenFromData(t *testing.T) {
    app := newIntegrationApp(t)
    coachNoLocation := app.seedUser(t, "coach", nil) // nil location

    // Use a report that coaches are normally allowed to access
    reportID := app.reportIDByType(t, "coach_schedule")

    rec := app.get(t, "/api/v1/reports/"+reportID.String()+"/data", app.login(t, coachNoLocation))
    requireStatus(t, rec, http.StatusForbidden)

    if decodeError(t, rec).Error.Code != "FORBIDDEN" {
        t.Fatal("expected FORBIDDEN for coach without assigned location")
    }
}
```

Note: if `reportIDByType` doesn't handle `"coach_schedule"` use `app.reportIDByType(t, "inventory_summary")` — what matters is reaching the scope-enforcement code in `GetReportData`, which runs after the role check.

---

## Issue 7 — [Low] reporting.go is a placeholder package

### Root cause

`repo/backend/internal/reporting/reporting.go` contains only a package-level doc comment
and no exported symbols. It is unreferenced by the rest of the application and adds noise.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/internal/reporting/reporting.go`** — The doc comment accurately describes
planned future work (Prompt 7). Add a compile guard so the package is never silently empty:

```go
// Package reporting provides KPI aggregation, report data generation, and
// export file creation for the FitCommerce platform.
// ... (keep existing doc comment) ...
package reporting

// Placeholder is a sentinel that prevents the package from being completely
// empty until the reporting implementations described in the package doc are
// added. Remove when the first real symbol is exported.
const Placeholder = true
```

---

## Files Modified

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go` | Return 403 when coach has nil locationID |
| `repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go` | Return 403 when coach has nil locationID |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts` | Extend OfflineMutationType with 8 new operation types |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useOrders.ts` | Enqueue cancel/pay mutations when offline |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useCampaigns.ts` | Enqueue join/cancel mutations when offline |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useProcurement.ts` | Enqueue create/approve/receive/void PO mutations when offline |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts` | Add replay cases for all 8 new types; expand invalidation |
| `repo/frontend/src/components/FailedOfflineMutationsAlert.tsx` | Use loadFailedOfflineMutations directly |
| `repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go` | Validate from/to date format; return 422 on bad input |
| `repo/docker-compose.yml` | Add ssl-certs volume; set FC_TLS_CERT_FILE/KEY on backend; mount to frontend |
| `repo/frontend/nginx.conf` | Enable proxy_ssl_verify; reference shared cert |
| `repo/backend/api_tests/dashboard_test.go` | Add coach-without-location 403 test |
| `repo/backend/api_tests/report_export_test.go` | Add coach-without-location 403 test |
| `repo/backend/internal/reporting/reporting.go` | Add Placeholder const to avoid empty package |

---

## Verification

```bash
cd repo/backend && go build ./...
```
Expect: no compile errors.

```bash
cd repo/backend && go test ./api_tests/... -run "TestDashboard_CoachWithoutLocation|TestReports_CoachWithoutLocation" -v
```
Expect: both new tests pass with 403.

```bash
cd repo/backend && go test ./unit_tests/... -v
```
Expect: all existing tests still pass.

Frontend changes (offline hooks, FailedOfflineMutationsAlert) are verified by existing
frontend unit tests and manual smoke-testing the alert component when failed mutations exist
in IndexedDB.
"}

`useReceivePO`, `useVoidPO`:

each new type, calling the corresponding `apiClient` endpoint:

## Issue 3 — [Medium] Dashboard date filters silently accept invalid date strings

### Root cause

`dashboard_service.go:76-77`: `curStart, _ = time.Parse("2006-01-02", from)` — parse error
silently discarded; `curStart` stays zero value, which causes incorrect period computations.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go`** — Validate `from` / `to` before calling
the service. After extracting `from` and `to` (around line 62-64):
```go
const dateLayout = "2006-01-02"
if from != "" {
    if _, err := time.Parse(dateLayout, from); err != nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, NewErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", "from must be a date in YYYY-MM-DD format"))
    }
}
if to != "" {
    if _, err := time.Parse(dateLayout, to); err != nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, NewErrorResponse("VALIDATION_ERROR", "to must be a date in YYYY-MM-DD format"))
    }
}
```

Add `"time"` to imports if not already present.

---

## Issue 4 — [Medium] nginx proxy_ssl_verify disabled

### Root cause

`nginx.conf:26`: `proxy_ssl_verify off;` disables certificate verification on the
frontend → backend proxy hop. Combined with `proxy_pass https://backend:8080/`, this
means the TLS connection is established without verifying the server identity.

### Fix

The backend generates a cert at startup via `EnsureServerTLSFiles`. In ephemeral mode it
writes to a temp dir (`fitcommerce-tls-*`). To let nginx verify the cert, we give the
backend a stable, shared cert path and mount it into nginx.

**`repo/docker-compose.yml`**:
- Add a `ssl-certs` named volume.
- Add to `backend` service:
  ```yaml
  volumes:
    - ssl-certs:/etc/ssl/fitcommerce
  environment:
    FC_TLS_CERT_FILE: "/etc/ssl/fitcommerce/server.crt"
    FC_TLS_KEY_FILE:  "/etc/ssl/fitcommerce/server.key"
  ```
- Add to `frontend` service:
  ```yaml
  volumes:
    - ssl-certs:/etc/nginx/backend-certs:ro
  ```
- Add volume declaration at bottom: `ssl-certs:`

**`repo/frontend/nginx.conf`** — Replace the TLS verification block:
```nginx
proxy_ssl_verify on;
proxy_ssl_trusted_certificate /etc/nginx/backend-certs/server.crt;
proxy_ssl_server_name on;
```
(Remove `proxy_ssl_verify off;`)

---

## Issue 5 — [Medium] FailedOfflineMutationsAlert always shows empty

### Root cause

`FailedOfflineMutationsAlert.tsx:18-19`: uses `loadPendingOfflineMutations()` then filters
for `status === 'failed'`, but `loadPendingOfflineMutations` already filters **out** failed
entries (returns only pending). The `.filter` always produces an empty array.

`loadFailedOfflineMutations` already exists in `offline-cache.ts:197-201` — use it directly.

### Fix

**`repo/frontend/src/components/FailedOfflineMutationsAlert.tsx`**:
- Change import: replace `loadPendingOfflineMutations` with `loadFailedOfflineMutations`
- Change `useEffect` body:
  ```ts
  loadFailedOfflineMutations().then(setFailedMutations);
  ```

---

## Issue 6 — [Medium] No API tests for coach-without-location scope

### Root cause

`dashboard_test.go` tests member forbidden and coach-with-location; no coach-without-location
test. `report_export_test.go` tests admin-only report forbidden; no coach-without-location test.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/api_tests/dashboard_test.go`** — Add:
```go
func TestDashboard_CoachWithoutLocationIsForbidden(t *testing.T) {
    app := newIntegrationApp(t)
    coach := app.seedUser(t, "coach", nil) // nil = no location assigned

    rec := app.get(t, "/api/v1/dashboard/kpis", app.login(t, coach))
    requireStatus(t, rec, http.StatusForbidden)

    if decodeError(t, rec).Error.Code != "FORBIDDEN" {
        t.Fatal("expected FORBIDDEN for coach without assigned location")
    }
}
```

**`repo/backend/api_tests/report_export_test.go`** — Add:
```go
func TestReports_CoachWithoutLocationIsForbiddenFromData(t *testing.T) {
    app := newIntegrationApp(t)
    coachNoLocation := app.seedUser(t, "coach", nil) // nil location

    // Use a report that coaches are normally allowed to access
    reportID := app.reportIDByType(t, "coach_schedule")

    rec := app.get(t, "/api/v1/reports/"+reportID.String()+"/data", app.login(t, coachNoLocation))
    requireStatus(t, rec, http.StatusForbidden)

    if decodeError(t, rec).Error.Code != "FORBIDDEN" {
        t.Fatal("expected FORBIDDEN for coach without assigned location")
    }
}
```

Note: if `reportIDByType` doesn't handle `"coach_schedule"` use `app.reportIDByType(t, "inventory_summary")` — what matters is reaching the scope-enforcement code in `GetReportData`, which runs after the role check.

## Issue 1 — [High] Coach without assigned location fails open

### Root cause

`dashboard_handler.go:44-46`: When coach has nil `LocationID`, `locationID = user.LocationID`
sets `locationID` to `nil`, removing all location scoping — the service returns aggregate
data across all locations.

`report_handler.go:83-85`: The scope injection guard `user.LocationID != nil` is correct
for normal coaches but silently skips enforcement when the coach has no assigned location,
returning unscoped data.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/internal/http/dashboard_handler.go`** — After the coach scope block (line 44):
```go
if user.Role == domain.UserRoleCoach {
    if user.LocationID == nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, NewErrorResponse("FORBIDDEN", "coach account has no assigned location"))
    }
    locationID = user.LocationID
}
```

**`repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go`** — Replace the existing coach scope block (line 83-85):
```go
if user.Role == domain.UserRoleCoach {
    if user.LocationID == nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, NewErrorResponse("FORBIDDEN", "coach account has no assigned location"))
    }
    filters["location_id"] = user.LocationID.String()
}
```

{"file_path": "C:\Users\Hello\.claude\plans\happy-doodling-cosmos.md", "content": "# Fix 4 Remaining Audit Issues – Fifth Round (2026-04-11)

## Context

A fifth audit pass found 4 issues remaining after the fourth-round fixes. The prior round
covered cancel/pay orders, join/cancel campaigns, create/approve/receive/void PO — but
missed 9 more operational mutations across 4 files. Tests for new replay paths are also absent.

---

## Issue 1 — [High] Offline-first still misses 9 operational mutations

### Missing operations and new mutation type names

| Hook | File | New type |
|---|---|---|
| `useRefundOrder` | useOrders.ts:97 | `"refund-order"` |
| `useAddOrderNote` | useOrders.ts:114 | `"add-order-note"` |
| `useSplitOrder` | useOrders.ts:125 | `"split-order"` |
| `useMergeOrder` | useOrders.ts:140 | `"merge-order"` |
| `useEvaluateCampaign` | useCampaigns.ts:94 | `"evaluate-campaign"` |
| `useCreateCampaign` | useCampaigns.ts:105 | `"create-campaign"` |
| `useCreateAdjustment` | useInventory.ts:45 | `"create-adjustment"` |
| `useReturnPO` | useProcurement.ts:202 | `"return-po"` |
| `useResolveVariance` | useProcurement.ts:252 | `"resolve-variance"` |

### Changes

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts`** — Append 9 types to `OfflineMutationType`:
```ts
| "refund-order"
| "add-order-note"
| "split-order"
| "merge-order"
| "evaluate-campaign"
| "create-campaign"
| "create-adjustment"
| "return-po"
| "resolve-variance"
```

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useOrders.ts`** — Update 4 hooks using same pattern as cancel/pay:
- `useRefundOrder`: enqueue `{ type: "refund-order", payload: { id } }`
- `useAddOrderNote`: enqueue `{ type: "add-order-note", payload: { id, note } }`
- `useSplitOrder`: enqueue `{ type: "split-order", payload: { id, quantities } }`
- `useMergeOrder`: enqueue `{ type: "merge-order", payload: { order_ids } }`

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useCampaigns.ts`** — Update 2 hooks:
- `useEvaluateCampaign`: enqueue `{ type: "evaluate-campaign", payload: { id } }`
- `useCreateCampaign`: enqueue `{ type: "create-campaign", payload: body as Record<string,unknown> }`

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useInventory.ts`** — Import `enqueueOfflineMutation`; update:
- `useCreateAdjustment`: enqueue `{ type: "create-adjustment", payload: payload as Record<string,unknown> }`

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useProcurement.ts`** — Update 2 hooks:
- `useReturnPO`: enqueue `{ type: "return-po", payload: { id } }`
- `useResolveVariance`: enqueue `{ type: "resolve-variance", payload: { id, action, resolution_notes, quantity_change } }`

**`repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts`** — Add 9 cases in `executeMutation`:
```ts
case "refund-order":
  await apiClient.post(`/orders/${entry.payload.id}/refund`, {});
  return;
case "add-order-note":
  await apiClient.post(`/orders/${entry.payload.id}/notes`, { note: entry.payload.note });
  return;
case "split-order":
  await apiClient.post(`/orders/${entry.payload.id}/split`, { quantities: entry.payload.quantities });
  return;
case "merge-order":
  await apiClient.post(`/orders/merge`, { order_ids: entry.payload.order_ids });
  return;
case "evaluate-campaign":
  await apiClient.post(`/campaigns/${entry.payload.id}/evaluate`, {});
  return;
case "create-campaign":
  await apiClient.post("/campaigns", entry.payload);
  return;
case "create-adjustment":
  await apiClient.post("/inventory/adjustments", entry.payload);
  return;
case "return-po":
  await apiClient.post(`/purchase-orders/${entry.payload.id}/return`, {});
  return;
case "resolve-variance":
  await apiClient.post(`/variances/${entry.payload.id}/resolve`, {
    action: entry.payload.action,
    resolution_notes: entry.payload.resolution_notes,
    quantity_change: entry.payload.quantity_change,
  });
  return;
```

Also add `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["inventory"] })` and
`queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["variances"] })` to the `replayOfflineMutations`
post-processing block.

---

## Issue 2 — [Medium] Replay test depth too narrow

### Fix

**`repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-mutations.test.ts`** — Add table-driven success tests
for each new mutation type. Use the existing mock pattern (`mockPost`, `mockLoadPending`,
`mockRemove`). Group by domain inside the existing `describe("replayOfflineMutations")` block:

Add one `it(...)` per new type asserting:
- `mockPost` called with the correct path + body
- `mockRemove` called with the entry id
- `processed` count increments

Representative examples (all follow the same structure):
```ts
it.each([
  ["refund-order",      { id: "o1" },                            "/orders/o1/refund",               {}],
  ["add-order-note",    { id: "o1", note: "hello" },             "/orders/o1/notes",                { note: "hello" }],
  ["split-order",       { id: "o1", quantities: [1,2] },         "/orders/o1/split",                { quantities: [1,2] }],
  ["merge-order",       { order_ids: ["o1","o2"] },              "/orders/merge",                   { order_ids: ["o1","o2"] }],
  ["evaluate-campaign", { id: "c1" },                            "/campaigns/c1/evaluate",          {}],
  ["create-campaign",   { item_id: "i1", min_quantity: 5, cutoff_time: "2026-06-01T00:00:00Z" },
                                                                  "/campaigns",                      { item_id: "i1", min_quantity: 5, cutoff_time: "2026-06-01T00:00:00Z" }],
  ["create-adjustment", { item_id: "i1", quantity_change: -2, reason: "damaged" },
                                                                  "/inventory/adjustments",          { item_id: "i1", quantity_change: -2, reason: "damaged" }],
  ["return-po",         { id: "po1" },                           "/purchase-orders/po1/return",     {}],
  ["resolve-variance",  { id: "v1", action: "adjustment", resolution_notes: "ok", quantity_change: 1 },
                                                                  "/variances/v1/resolve",           { action: "adjustment", resolution_notes: "ok", quantity_change: 1 }],
])("replays %s mutation", async (type, payload, expectedPath, expectedBody) => {
  mockLoadPending.mockResolvedValue([{ id: "q1", type, payload, createdAt: 1, status: "pending" }]);
  mockPost.mockResolvedValue({});
  const qc = new QueryClient();
  const count = await replayOfflineMutations(qc);
  expect(count).toBe(1);
  expect(mockPost).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expectedPath, expectedBody);
  expect(mockRemove).toHaveBeenCalledWith("q1");
});
```

---

## Issue 3 — [Medium] No API test for invalid dashboard date filters

### Fix

**`repo/backend/api_tests/dashboard_test.go`** — Add:
```go
func TestDashboard_InvalidDateFiltersReturn422(t *testing.T) {
    app := newIntegrationApp(t)
    admin := app.seedUser(t, "administrator", nil)
    cookies := app.login(t, admin)

    badFrom := app.get(t, "/api/v1/dashboard/kpis?from=not-a-date", cookies)
    requireStatus(t, badFrom, http.StatusUnprocessableEntity)
    if decodeError(t, badFrom).Error.Code != "VALIDATION_ERROR" {
        t.Fatal("expected VALIDATION_ERROR for invalid from date")
    }

    badTo := app.get(t, "/api/v1/dashboard/kpis?to=31-01-2026", cookies)
    requireStatus(t, badTo, http.StatusUnprocessableEntity)
    if decodeError(t, badTo).Error.Code != "VALIDATION_ERROR" {
        t.Fatal("expected VALIDATION_ERROR for invalid to date")
    }
}
```

---

## Issue 4 — [Low] reporting.go still adds architectural ambiguity

### Fix

The misleading doc comment implies full implementations are present; `Placeholder = true`
adds no clarity. Replace the entire file with a minimal honest declaration:

**`repo/backend/internal/reporting/reporting.go`** — Overwrite with:
```go
// Package reporting is reserved for future implementation.
// No symbols are exported yet.
package reporting
```

This removes the false promise while keeping the package namespace reserved.

---

## Files Modified

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-cache.ts` | Add 9 new mutation types |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useOrders.ts` | Enqueue refund/note/split/merge offline |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useCampaigns.ts` | Enqueue evaluate/create offline |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useInventory.ts` | Enqueue create-adjustment offline |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/hooks/useProcurement.ts` | Enqueue return/resolve-variance offline |
| `repo/frontend/src/lib/offline-mutations.ts` | Add 9 replay cases + inventory/variance invalidation |
| `repo/frontend/unit_tests/lib/offline-mutations.test.ts` | Add table-driven test for all 9 new types |
| `repo/backend/api_tests/dashboard_test.go` | Add 422 test for invalid date params |
| `repo/backend/internal/reporting/reporting.go` | Replace with minimal honest package declaration |

---

## Verification

```bash
cd repo/backend && go build ./...
```
Expect: no errors.

```bash
cd repo/backend && go test ./api_tests/... -run "TestDashboard_InvalidDate" -v
```
Expect: passes with 422.
"}

- `useCreateCampaign`: enqueue `{ type: "create-campaign", payload: body as Record<string,unknown> }`

- `useResolveVariance`: enqueue `{ type: "resolve-variance", payload: { id, action, resolution_notes, quantity_change } }`

```ts
case "refund-order":
  await apiClient.post(`/orders/${entry.payload.id}/refund`, {});
  return;
case "add-order-note":
  await apiClient.post(`/orders/${entry.payload.id}/notes`, { note: entry.payload.note });
  return;

  });
  return;
```

```go
func TestDashboard_InvalidDateFiltersReturn422(t *testing.T) {
    app := newIntegrationApp(t)
    admin := app.seedUser(t, "administrator", nil)
    cookies := app.login(t, admin)

    badFrom := app.get(t, "/api/v1/dashboard/kpis?from=not-a-date", cookies)
    requireStatus(t, badFrom, http.StatusUnprocessableEntity)
    if decodeError(t, badFrom).Error.Code != "VALIDATION_ERROR" {
        t.Fatal("expected VALIDATION_ERROR for invalid from date")
    }

    badTo := app.get(t, "/api/v1/dashboard/kpis?to=31-01-2026", cookies)
    requireStatus(t, badTo, http.StatusUnprocessableEntity)
    if decodeError(t, badTo).Error.Code != "VALIDATION_ERROR" {
        t.Fatal("expected VALIDATION_ERROR for invalid to date")
    }
}
```

{"file_path": "C:\Users\Hello\.claude\plans\happy-doodling-cosmos.md", "content": "# Fix 2 High-Severity Issues – Sixth Round (2026-04-11)

## Context

Hooks now queue mutations when offline, but 5 page files still guard action buttons with
`disabled={isOffline}` — so the queue code is unreachable from the UI. Separately,
`RunExport` never got the coach location-scope guard added to `GetReportData` in an
earlier round, leaving a second path for coaches to obtain un-scoped export data.

---

## Issue 1 — Offline-disabled UI controls block queue-capable mutations

### Root cause summary

Every page that calls a queue-capable mutation has:
1. `disabled={isOffline}` (or `|| isOffline`) on the submit / action button → user can't click
2. `if (!id || isOffline) return;` guard in the inline handler → even if clicked, the call exits early

Both must be removed. After removal, the hook's offline path runs, enqueues the mutation, and
returns `undefined`. The handler should then show a "queued" notification instead of "success".

The inline dialog offline `<Alert>` blocks (showing `OFFLINE_MUTATION_MESSAGE`) must also
change from a warning that implies the action is blocked, to an informational note that it
will be queued — so users understand the action is accepted, not prevented.

### Pattern applied uniformly across all five pages

**Button disables to remove:**
- Remove `disabled={isOffline}` from standalone action buttons
- Remove `|| isOffline` from compound `disabled={... || isOffline}` expressions on submit buttons

**Handler guards to remove:**
- Change `if (!id || isOffline) return;` → `if (!id) return;`

**Success message update:**
- After `await mutation.mutateAsync(...)`, check `isOffline`:
  - offline → `notify.info('Action queued — will sync when you reconnect.')`
  - online → existing success message unchanged

**Dialog offline alerts:**
- Change `<Alert severity="warning">{OFFLINE_MUTATION_MESSAGE}</Alert>`
  to `<Alert severity="info">Offline — this action will be queued and applied when you reconnect.</Alert>`

### File-by-file changes

---

#### `repo/frontend/src/routes/OrderDetailPage.tsx`

**`AddNoteDialog` (line ~109):**
```tsx
// Change:
disabled={addNoteMutation.isPending || !note.trim() || isOffline}
// To:
disabled={addNoteMutation.isPending || !note.trim()}
```
Alert in AddNoteDialog body (line ~86-90): change severity to `"info"`, message to queued text.
`handleSubmit` (line ~70-80): after mutateAsync, check `isOffline` → `notify.info(...)` vs `notify.success("Note added.")`.

**`PayDialog` (line ~176):**
```tsx
// Change:
disabled={payMutation.isPending || !marker.trim() || isOffline}
// To:
disabled={payMutation.isPending || !marker.trim()}
```
Alert (line ~154-158): change to info + queued text.
`handleSubmit` (line ~138-148): check `isOffline` after success.

**`SplitDialog` (line ~284):**
```tsx
// Change:
disabled={splitMutation.isPending || !isValid || isOffline}
// To:
disabled={splitMutation.isPending || !isValid}
```
Alert (line ~256-260): change to info + queued text.
`handleSubmit` (line ~241-250): check `isOffline` after success.

**Page-level action buttons (lines ~391–441):**
```tsx
// Split button:
disabled={isOffline}  → remove prop entirely

// Cancel Order button:
disabled={isOffline}  → remove

// Refund button:
disabled={isOffline}  → remove

// Record Payment button:
disabled={isOffline}  → remove

// Add Note button:
disabled={isOffline}  → remove
```

**Inline handlers (lines ~336-356):**
```tsx
// handleCancel:
if (!id || isOffline) return;  →  if (!id) return;
notify.success("Order cancelled.")  →  isOffline ? notify.info("Action queued ...") : notify.success("Order cancelled.")

// handleRefund:
if (!id || isOffline) return;  →  if (!id) return;
notify.success("Order refunded.")  →  isOffline ? notify.info("Action queued ...") : notify.success("Order refunded.")
```

---

#### `repo/frontend/src/routes/GroupBuyDetailPage.tsx`

**Action buttons (lines ~130-146):**
```tsx
// Evaluate button:  disabled={isOffline}  → remove
// Cancel button:    disabled={isOffline}  → remove
```

**Join form (lines ~240-270):**
```tsx
// Alert: change severity + message to queued info text
// Join button:
disabled={isSubmitting || joinMutation.isPending || isOffline}  →  disabled={isSubmitting || joinMutation.isPending}
```

**Handlers `handleCancel`, `handleEvaluate`, `handleJoin` (lines ~57-89):**
No isOffline guards to remove here. Add `isOffline` check after each `mutateAsync` to select
`notify.info(queued)` vs the existing success message.

---

#### `repo/frontend/src/routes/GroupBuysPage.tsx`

**`CreateCampaignDialog` submit button (line ~173):**
```tsx
disabled={isSubmitting || isOffline}  →  disabled={isSubmitting}
```
Alert (line ~132-134): change to info + queued text.
`onSubmit` (line ~104-117): add `isOffline` check after mutateAsync to show queued vs success.

**Page-level "Create Campaign" button (line ~275):**
```tsx
disabled={isOffline}  → remove prop
```

---

#### `repo/frontend/src/routes/InventoryPage.tsx`

**`CreateAdjustmentDialog` submit button (line ~135):**
```tsx
disabled={isSubmitting || isOffline}  →  disabled={isSubmitting}
```
Alert (line ~97-99): change to info + queued text.
`onSubmit` (line ~79-88): add `isOffline` check after mutateAsync.

**Page-level "Create Adjustment" button (line ~173):**
```tsx
disabled={isOffline}  → remove prop
```

---

#### `repo/frontend/src/routes/PurchaseOrderDetailPage.tsx`

**`ResolveVarianceDialog` submit button (line ~261):**
```tsx
disabled={resolveMutation.isPending || !notes.trim() || (action === 'adjustment' && !quantityChange.trim()) || isOffline}
→ remove || isOffline
```

**Page-level action buttons (lines ~373-399):**
```tsx
// Approve: disabled={approveMutation.isPending || isOffline}  → remove || isOffline
// Receive: disabled={isOffline}  → remove
// Return:  disabled={isOffline}  → remove
// Void:    disabled={isOffline}  → remove
```

**Inline handlers `handleApprove`, `handleReturn`, `handleVoid` (lines ~291-321):**
```tsx
if (!id || isOffline) return;  →  if (!id) return;
// After each mutateAsync success, add isOffline-aware message
```

---

## Issue 2 — RunExport lacks coach location scope guard

### Root cause

`report_handler.go:RunExport` calls `h.svc.GenerateExport(...)` without applying the
coach location scope enforcement that `GetReportData` applies (lines 83-85). A coach with
an assigned location can supply arbitrary `parameters["location_id"]` in the POST body
and receive export data for any location. A coach without a location must be denied.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go`** — after `uuid.Parse(req.ReportID)` and
before calling `h.svc.GenerateExport`, add:

```go
// Coaches are scoped to their assigned location — same enforcement as GetReportData.
if user.Role == domain.UserRoleCoach {
    if user.LocationID == nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, NewErrorResponse("FORBIDDEN", "coach account has no assigned location"))
    }
    if req.Parameters == nil {
        req.Parameters = make(map[string]string)
    }
    req.Parameters["location_id"] = user.LocationID.String()
}
```

`req.Parameters` is `map[string]string` (from `dto.CreateExportRequest`) — set directly,
no service-layer change needed.

---

## Files Modified

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/OrderDetailPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables + guards; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/GroupBuyDetailPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/GroupBuysPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/InventoryPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/PurchaseOrderDetailPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables + guards; add queued notification |
| `repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go` | Add coach location scope to RunExport |

---

## Verification

```bash
cd repo/backend && go build ./...
```
Expect: no errors.

Frontend: all action buttons are enabled regardless of `isOffline`; mutations enqueue when
offline; `notify.info("Action queued — will sync when you reconnect.")` appears when
offline and a button is pressed.

Coach export scope: a coach with a location can only export data for their own location;
a coach without a location receives 403 on POST /exports.
"}

```go
    }
    if req.Parameters == nil {

    }

}
```

no service-layer change needed.

{"allowedPrompts": [{"tool": "Bash", "prompt": "run go build"}], "plan": "# Fix 2 High-Severity Issues – Sixth Round (2026-04-11)

## Context

Hooks now queue mutations when offline, but 5 page files still guard action buttons with
`disabled={isOffline}` — so the queue code is unreachable from the UI. Separately,
`RunExport` never got the coach location-scope guard added to `GetReportData` in an
earlier round, leaving a second path for coaches to obtain un-scoped export data.

---

## Issue 1 — Offline-disabled UI controls block queue-capable mutations

### Root cause summary

Every page that calls a queue-capable mutation has:
1. `disabled={isOffline}` (or `|| isOffline`) on the submit / action button → user can't click
2. `if (!id || isOffline) return;` guard in the inline handler → even if clicked, the call exits early

Both must be removed. After removal, the hook's offline path runs, enqueues the mutation, and
returns `undefined`. The handler should then show a "queued" notification instead of "success".

The inline dialog offline `<Alert>` blocks (showing `OFFLINE_MUTATION_MESSAGE`) must also
change from a warning that implies the action is blocked, to an informational note that it
will be queued — so users understand the action is accepted, not prevented.

### Pattern applied uniformly across all five pages

**Button disables to remove:**
- Remove `disabled={isOffline}` from standalone action buttons
- Remove `|| isOffline` from compound `disabled={... || isOffline}` expressions on submit buttons

**Handler guards to remove:**
- Change `if (!id || isOffline) return;` → `if (!id) return;`

**Success message update:**
- After `await mutation.mutateAsync(...)`, check `isOffline`:
  - offline → `notify.info('Action queued — will sync when you reconnect.')`
  - online → existing success message unchanged

**Dialog offline alerts:**
- Change `<Alert severity="warning">{OFFLINE_MUTATION_MESSAGE}</Alert>`
  to `<Alert severity="info">Offline — this action will be queued and applied when you reconnect.</Alert>`

### File-by-file changes

---

#### `repo/frontend/src/routes/OrderDetailPage.tsx`

**`AddNoteDialog` (line ~109):**
```tsx
// Change:
disabled={addNoteMutation.isPending || !note.trim() || isOffline}
// To:
disabled={addNoteMutation.isPending || !note.trim()}
```
Alert in AddNoteDialog body (line ~86-90): change severity to `"info"`, message to queued text.
`handleSubmit` (line ~70-80): after mutateAsync, check `isOffline` → `notify.info(...)` vs `notify.success("Note added.")`.

**`PayDialog` (line ~176):**
```tsx
// Change:
disabled={payMutation.isPending || !marker.trim() || isOffline}
// To:
disabled={payMutation.isPending || !marker.trim()}
```
Alert (line ~154-158): change to info + queued text.
`handleSubmit` (line ~138-148): check `isOffline` after success.

**`SplitDialog` (line ~284):**
```tsx
// Change:
disabled={splitMutation.isPending || !isValid || isOffline}
// To:
disabled={splitMutation.isPending || !isValid}
```
Alert (line ~256-260): change to info + queued text.
`handleSubmit` (line ~241-250): check `isOffline` after success.

**Page-level action buttons (lines ~391–441):**
```tsx
// Split button:
disabled={isOffline}  → remove prop entirely

// Cancel Order button:
disabled={isOffline}  → remove

// Refund button:
disabled={isOffline}  → remove

// Record Payment button:
disabled={isOffline}  → remove

// Add Note button:
disabled={isOffline}  → remove
```

**Inline handlers (lines ~336-356):**
```tsx
// handleCancel:
if (!id || isOffline) return;  →  if (!id) return;
notify.success("Order cancelled.")  →  isOffline ? notify.info("Action queued ...") : notify.success("Order cancelled.")

// handleRefund:
if (!id || isOffline) return;  →  if (!id) return;
notify.success("Order refunded.")  →  isOffline ? notify.info("Action queued ...") : notify.success("Order refunded.")
```

---

#### `repo/frontend/src/routes/GroupBuyDetailPage.tsx`

**Action buttons (lines ~130-146):**
```tsx
// Evaluate button:  disabled={isOffline}  → remove
// Cancel button:    disabled={isOffline}  → remove
```

**Join form (lines ~240-270):**
```tsx
// Alert: change severity + message to queued info text
// Join button:
disabled={isSubmitting || joinMutation.isPending || isOffline}  →  disabled={isSubmitting || joinMutation.isPending}
```

**Handlers `handleCancel`, `handleEvaluate`, `handleJoin` (lines ~57-89):**
No isOffline guards to remove here. Add `isOffline` check after each `mutateAsync` to select
`notify.info(queued)` vs the existing success message.

---

#### `repo/frontend/src/routes/GroupBuysPage.tsx`

**`CreateCampaignDialog` submit button (line ~173):**
```tsx
disabled={isSubmitting || isOffline}  →  disabled={isSubmitting}
```
Alert (line ~132-134): change to info + queued text.
`onSubmit` (line ~104-117): add `isOffline` check after mutateAsync to show queued vs success.

**Page-level "Create Campaign" button (line ~275):**
```tsx
disabled={isOffline}  → remove prop
```

---

#### `repo/frontend/src/routes/InventoryPage.tsx`

**`CreateAdjustmentDialog` submit button (line ~135):**
```tsx
disabled={isSubmitting || isOffline}  →  disabled={isSubmitting}
```
Alert (line ~97-99): change to info + queued text.
`onSubmit` (line ~79-88): add `isOffline` check after mutateAsync.

**Page-level "Create Adjustment" button (line ~173):**
```tsx
disabled={isOffline}  → remove prop
```

---

#### `repo/frontend/src/routes/PurchaseOrderDetailPage.tsx`

**`ResolveVarianceDialog` submit button (line ~261):**
```tsx
disabled={resolveMutation.isPending || !notes.trim() || (action === 'adjustment' && !quantityChange.trim()) || isOffline}
→ remove || isOffline
```

**Page-level action buttons (lines ~373-399):**
```tsx
// Approve: disabled={approveMutation.isPending || isOffline}  → remove || isOffline
// Receive: disabled={isOffline}  → remove
// Return:  disabled={isOffline}  → remove
// Void:    disabled={isOffline}  → remove
```

**Inline handlers `handleApprove`, `handleReturn`, `handleVoid` (lines ~291-321):**
```tsx
if (!id || isOffline) return;  →  if (!id) return;
// After each mutateAsync success, add isOffline-aware message
```

---

## Issue 2 — RunExport lacks coach location scope guard

### Root cause

`report_handler.go:RunExport` calls `h.svc.GenerateExport(...)` without applying the
coach location scope enforcement that `GetReportData` applies (lines 83-85). A coach with
an assigned location can supply arbitrary `parameters["location_id"]` in the POST body
and receive export data for any location. A coach without a location must be denied.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go`** — after `uuid.Parse(req.ReportID)` and
before calling `h.svc.GenerateExport`, add:

```go
// Coaches are scoped to their assigned location — same enforcement as GetReportData.
if user.Role == domain.UserRoleCoach {
    if user.LocationID == nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, NewErrorResponse("FORBIDDEN", "coach account has no assigned location"))
    }
    if req.Parameters == nil {
        req.Parameters = make(map[string]string)
    }
    req.Parameters["location_id"] = user.LocationID.String()
}
```

`req.Parameters` is `map[string]string` (from `dto.CreateExportRequest`) — set directly,
no service-layer change needed.

---

## Files Modified

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/OrderDetailPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables + guards; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/GroupBuyDetailPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/GroupBuysPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/InventoryPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/PurchaseOrderDetailPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables + guards; add queued notification |
| `repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go` | Add coach location scope to RunExport |

---

## Verification

```bash
cd repo/backend && go build ./...
```
Expect: no errors.

Frontend: all action buttons are enabled regardless of `isOffline`; mutations enqueue when
offline; `notify.info("Action queued — will sync when you reconnect.")` appears when
offline and a button is pressed.

Coach export scope: a coach with a location can only export data for their own location;
a coach without a location receives 403 on POST /exports.

an assigned location can supply arbitrary `parameters["location_id"]` in the POST body
and receive export data for any location. A coach without a location must be denied.

### Fix

**`repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go`** — after `uuid.Parse(req.ReportID)` and
before calling `h.svc.GenerateExport`, add:

```go
// Coaches are scoped to their assigned location — same enforcement as GetReportData.
if user.Role == domain.UserRoleCoach {
    if user.LocationID == nil {
        return c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, NewErrorResponse("FORBIDDEN", "coach account has no assigned location"))
    }
    if req.Parameters == nil {
        req.Parameters = make(map[string]string)
    }
    req.Parameters["location_id"] = user.LocationID.String()
}
```

`req.Parameters` is `map[string]string` (from `dto.CreateExportRequest`) — set directly,
no service-layer change needed.

---

| `repo/frontend/src/routes/OrderDetailPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables + guards; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/GroupBuyDetailPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/GroupBuysPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/InventoryPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables; add queued notification |
| `repo/frontend/src/routes/PurchaseOrderDetailPage.tsx` | Remove offline disables + guards; add queued notification |
| `repo/backend/internal/http/report_handler.go` | Add coach location scope to RunExport |

```bash
cd repo/backend && go build ./...
```

Frontend: all action buttons are enabled regardless of `isOffline`; mutations enqueue when
offline; `notify.info("Action queued — will sync when you reconnect.")` appears when
offline and a button is pressed.

Coach export scope: a coach with a location can only export data for their own location;
a coach without a location receives 403 on POST /exports.

All changes are complete. Here's a summary of what was done in this sixth round:

**Issue 1 — Offline-disabled UI controls (5 frontend files)**

Across `OrderDetailPage.tsx`, `GroupBuyDetailPage.tsx`, `GroupBuysPage.tsx`, `InventoryPage.tsx`, and `PurchaseOrderDetailPage.tsx`:

- Removed all `disabled={isOffline}` and `|| isOffline` from every queue-capable action button (14 total button changes)
- Removed all `if (!id || isOffline) return;` early-exit guards from inline handlers → `if (!id) return;` (5 handlers: handleCancel/handleRefund in OrderDetail, handleApprove/handleReturn/handleVoid in PODetail)
- Updated every mutation success handler to show `notify.info("Action queued — will sync when you reconnect.")` when offline, original success message when online
- Changed all `<Alert severity="warning">{OFFLINE_MUTATION_MESSAGE}</Alert>` inside dialogs to `<Alert severity="info">Offline — this action will be queued and applied when you reconnect.</Alert>` (6 dialog alerts)
- Removed the now-unused `OFFLINE_MUTATION_MESSAGE` import from all 5 files

**Issue 2 — RunExport missing coach location scope guard**

`report_handler.go:RunExport` — added the same coach location enforcement already present in `GetReportData`: 403 for coaches without an assigned location, and automatic injection of `location_id` into export parameters for coaches with one.

127	  - Renewal rate uses approximation without historical period baseline: backend/internal/application/dashboard_service.go:185.
128	- Impact: Dashboard decisions can be materially misleading, undermining core KPI-hub acceptance criteria.
129	- Minimum actionable fix: Introduce class/session attendance model and periodized KPI aggregation tables or query logic; enforce all declared filters per KPI; remove approximation shortcuts for renewal/productivity.
130	
131	- Severity: High
132	- Title: Fulfillment grouping dimensions are supported in backend but not fully exposed in frontend split/merge operations
133	- Conclusion: Partial fail for fulfillment workflow completeness
134	- Evidence:
135	  - Backend requests accept supplier_id/warehouse_bin_id/pickup_point: backend/internal/http/dto/requests.go:195, backend/internal/http/dto/requests.go:205.
136	  - Frontend merge dialog only confirms merge count, no fulfillment fields: frontend/src/routes/OrdersPage.tsx:60.

139	- Impact: Staff cannot fully execute prompt-required split/merge grouping by supplier/bin/pickup from the delivered UI.
140	- Minimum actionable fix: Extend split/merge dialogs and hooks to capture and submit optional supplier/bin/pickup fields; add form validation and timeline visibility for grouping context.
141	
142	- Severity: Medium

144	- Conclusion: Partial pass
145	- Evidence: backend/internal/jobs/procurement_jobs.go:20, backend/internal/jobs/procurement_jobs.go:28.
146	- Impact: Backups may drift relative to business-defined nightly window after restarts or long uptime variations.
147	- Minimum actionable fix: Add configurable scheduled execution (timezone-aware daily trigger time) instead of fixed 24h tick from process start.
148	
149	- Severity: Medium

158	6. Security Review Summary
159	
160	- Authentication entry points

162	  - Evidence and reasoning: Login/logout/session/captcha handlers and auth service implement session-cookie auth, lockout, CAPTCHA verification, and token non-disclosure in body. backend/internal/http/auth_handler.go:24, backend/internal/application/auth_service.go:40, backend/api_tests/lockout_test.go:58.

Broad object-level checks across all entity types are not fully proven in reviewed scope.

1	package application
